abstract_ts: The commercial availability of conductive materials and multi-material printers for fused filament fabrication (FFF) has paved the way for cost-effective and customizable sensors. While several studies have demonstrated the feasibility of sensors produced by this fabrication method, they typically require post-processing. We present a sensor design for single-step multiaxial force measurement encapsulated in regular printed FFF parts, relying exclusively on commercially available conductive and regular PLA filaments. The sensor employs a Maltese cross deformation structure with eight embedded strain-sensitive grids arranged in four half-bridge configurations, providing four output channels. Reliable electrical contact at critical transitions is ensured by a design-level partitioning strategy that prescribes toolpath generation of conductive parts, thereby improving manufacturing repeatability. A 5 N sensor is successfully fabricated and encapsulated without post-processing. Experimental characterization using a universal testing machine demonstrates a small linearity error of only 2.7% and clear decoupling of spatial force components using a calibration matrix obtained via ridge regression. This work enables reliable, low-cost fabrication of multiaxial sensors for integration into functional printed parts, offering a transferable and scalable solution for in-situ measurements such as in robotics, prosthetics or condition monitoring.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a <tex>$128 \times 128$</tex> CMOS microelectrode array for highly sensitive impedance biosensing. Conventional platforms showed insufficient spatial resolution and poor signal-to-noise ratios, limiting detection sensitivity through bulk measurements. The proposed CMOS platform integrates 16,384 individually addressable microelectrodes with dedicated amplification circuitry, enabling a shift from bulk measurements to discrete microparticle counting. The system operates across 20-100 kHz with modular architecture comprising a CMOS array chip, external MCU, and AD5933 impedance analyzer. Experimental validation demonstrates quantitative correlation between microparticle concentration and impedance change across multiple orders of magnitude (<tex>$1.02 \times 10^{6}$</tex> to <tex>$1.02 \times 10^{9}$</tex> particles/mL), with surface coverage from 0.25% to 98.65%. Dual-measurement approach combining electrical characterization with optical microscopy validates sensor accuracy. The CMOS impedance sensor achieves single-particle detection with real-time spatial localization, establishing a foundation for digital biosensing at ultra-low concentrations for point-of-care diagnostics and personalized medicine.

abstract_ts: Capacitive eye tracking has the potential to replace optical technologies for continuous low-power gaze detection in smart eyeglasses. In this work, different sensing approaches suitable for wearable integration are investigated. Starting from finite-element simulations, a detailed numerical electrical model was improved by comparing the simulated results with experimental measurements performed in different electrode configurations and charge-sensing circuits. Preliminary results show potential for the identification of a few regions of interest in the visual field with fF capacitive resolution.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a one-port microwave sensor designed for the detection of impurities in wine, particularly during the final bottling processes of wine production. The system comprises a microstrip-based resonant structure integrated with a mechanical assembly that securely holds a shielded thermoplastic polyurethane (TPU) tube through which the liquid under analysis flows and a post-processing stage to increase the sensitivity. The prototype has demonstrated the capability to detect residual water and detergent in white wine, as well as cross-contamination between different wine typeswhite wine in rosé wine and rosé wine in red wine-following the typical cleaning sequence employed in bottling facilities. The proposed system offers a non-invasive, real-time solution for quality control in wine production environments with a maximum sensitivity of 2.053% and 0.442 dB/%.

abstract_ts: The present research presents an implementation with hardware-in-the-loop of a previously proposed consensus-based time synchronization algorithm. This algorithm avoids the need for a master and a hierarchically fixed network, which is common in traditional synchronization systems. This in turn facilitates a retention of synchronization even in the case of dynamically changing networks. The demonstrator shows the viability of the basic concept of the algorithm. It hence still operates based on a common, periodic trigger prompting all devices to perform the synchronization. Based on the theoretical explanation of the algorithm, the respective structure of the demonstrator is outlined. In this context, an adapted algorithm to account for variations of this trigger, which are ubiquitous in physical clocks, is implemented. The results obtained with the demonstrator coincide with the theoretical behavior.

abstract_ts: In this paper, we present a radar-LiDAR fusion framework. The proposed method generates high-quality radar point clouds through joint optimization of the detection threshold and transformation parameters to align two sensors. It provides a lightweight and practical solution that does not rely on large-scale training data. Since the proposed method does not require preprocessing for extrinsic calibration between the two sensors, it can simplify the overall process of radar-LiDAR fusion framework. The geometric alignment between radar and LiDAR point clouds is evaluated by the Chamfer Distance, and our framework is validated with real-world sensor data showing that improved radar point clouds closely match LiDAR point clouds.

abstract_ts: Cross-sensitivity have long been a critical challenge in the field of CMOS gas sensors. Conventional approaches typically employ array-based configurations to construct gas detection systems to address this issue. However, the high sample demand and prolonged duration of a single-sample acquisition cycle significantly increase the burden of quantitative analysis experiments. Based on active learning, we developed two strategies by leveraging the geometric characteristics of gas sensor data in high-dimensional manifolds. Through prior assessment of the anomaly degree of sampling objects and manual guidance of sampling decision boundaries, this method effectively avoids oscillations in active learning query strategies, accelerates convergence speed, and reduces the time required for the sampling process in the joint calibration of the gas sensor array.

abstract_ts: Manual asset onboarding in large-scale retail operations presents a critical bottleneck, creating costly data inaccuracies that compromise preventive maintenance, capital planning, and overall efficiency. To address this, we introduce a novel, multi-stage hybrid AI framework that automates and enhances attribute extraction from asset nameplates, providing a robust and scalable alternative to error-prone manual data entry. A foundational component of our methodology is a predictive image quality model. This model, trained on 12 distinct featuresincluding contrast ratio, edge clarity, and wear pattern detectionacts as a critical gatekeeper to mitigate downstream errors and ensure cost-efficient processing. Following image quality assessment, an object detection model precisely localizes distinct regions of interest: the primary nameplate, QR codes, and circuit tag IDs. This targeted approach allows for the deployment of specialized tools, using dedicated libraries for high-fidelity QR code reading and optical character recognition (OCR) for circuit tag inference. The localized nameplate data are then processed by an advanced multimodal large language model (LLM) for more nuanced tasks, such as context-sensitive keyvalue extraction and brand name standardization. Furthermore, we demonstrate that a state-of-the-art pretrained multimodal model can automate asset class identification directly from the image, a task traditionally requiring manual intervention. As a final step, we implement fuzzy matching to standardize the variant of brand name entries, and implement the data validations. Performance is rigorously benchmarked using detailed, field-level metrics like Normalized Levenshtein Similarity and a comparative analysis of different LLMs on extraction accuracy and inference latency. This research delivers a comprehensive blueprint for an intelligent, hybrid automation system that promises a significant increase in data integrity and operational performance for asset lifecycle management.

abstract_ts: Prognostics and Health Management (PHM) is an emerging field which has received much attention from the manufacturing industry because of the benefits and efficiencies it brings to the table. And Remaining Useful Life (RUL) prediction is at the heart of any PHM system. Most recent data-driven research demand substantial volumes of labelled training data before a performant model can be trained under the supervised learning paradigm. This is where Transfer Learning (TL) and Domain Adaptation (DA) methods step in and make it possible for us to generalize a supervised model to other domains with different data distributions with no labelled data. In this paper, we propose LAMA-Net, an encoder-decoder based model (Transformer) with an induced bottleneck, Latent Alignment using Maximum Mean Discrepancy (MMD) and manifold learning is proposed to tackle the problem of Unsupervised Homogeneous Domain Adaptation for RUL prediction. LAMA-Net is validated using the C-MAPSS Turbofan Engine dataset by NASA and compared against other state-of-the-art techniques for DA. The results suggest that the proposed method offers a promising approach to perform domain adaptation in RUL prediction.

abstract_ts: Agentic memory is emerging as a key enabler for large language models (LLM) to maintain continuity, personalization, and long-term context in extended user interactions, critical capabilities for deploying LLMs as truly interactive and adaptive agents. Agentic memory refers to the memory that provides an LLM with agent-like persistence: the ability to retain and act upon information across conversations, similar to how a human would. We present Memoria, a modular memory framework that augments LLM-based conversational systems with persistent, interpretable, and context-rich memory. Memoria integrates two complementary components: dynamic session-level summarization and a weighted knowledge graph (KG)-based user modeling engine that incrementally captures user traits, preferences, and behavioral patterns as structured entities and relationships. This hybrid architecture enables both short-term dialogue coherence and long-term personalization while operating within the token constraints of modern LLMs. We demonstrate how Memoria enables scalable, personalized conversational artificial intelligence (AI) by bridging the gap between stateless LLM interfaces and agentic memory systems, offering a practical solution for industry applications requiring adaptive and evolving user experiences.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a fault-tolerant model predictive control (FCS-MPC) strategy for a three-level Active Neutral Point Clamped (ANPC) inverter, addressing challenges related to midpoint voltage imbalance and switch open-circuit faults. A novel cost function based on adaptive sigmoid weighting is introduced, enabling simultaneous optimization of current tracking, midpoint voltage balancing, and switching transitions. The proposed method identifies and masks faulted switching states in real-time, ensuring safe operation under single-switch open-circuit faults in phase A. Simulation results validate the controller’s effectiveness in both healthy and faulty conditions, demonstrating superior performance in current quality, midpoint voltage stability, and fault tolerance compared to conventional MPC approaches.

abstract_ts: With the integration of large-scale renewable energy, the uncertainty sources have significantly intensified in power systems. This paper presents a probabilistic optimal power flow (POPF) calculation method based on the Hybrid Discrete Fourier Transformation Matrix (HDFTM), through combining both sine and cosine samples as the sample matrix. In this way, the proposed HDFTM method effectively balances the trade-off between accuracy and efficiency observed in the two sub-methods of the conventional Discrete Fourier Transformation matrix Method, leading to an overall improvement in POPF computational performance. The superiority of the proposed method is illustrated in a modified IEEE 118-bus power system with Monte Carlo simulation method providing the benchmark.

abstract_ts: Effective monitoring of the operational status of secondary electrical equipment in thermal power plants is crucial for ensuring the stability and safety of the power system. Traditional manual monitoring and regular inspections face issues such as low efficiency and poor real-time performance. To address this, the paper proposes a method for monitoring the operational status of secondary electrical equipment in thermal power plants based on an improved Support Vector Machine (SVM) model. First, historical data from the operational equipment is collected, and preprocessing steps such as data cleaning, normalization, and feature extraction are applied. Next, building on the traditional SVM model, a genetic algorithm is used to optimize the model's penalty parameter <tex>$\boldsymbol{C}$</tex> and kernel function <tex>$\gamma$</tex>, enhancing the monitoring accuracy. Comparative results shows that the optimized SVM model improves the monitoring accuracy by 11.3%. Finally, case study validation demonstrates that the proposed method can successfully monitor the operational status of secondary equipment, with an identification accuracy of 95.1%, thus confirming the effectiveness of the method presented in this paper.

abstract_ts: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is a major neurodegenerative disorder, and early diagnosis is essential for effective intervention. However, centralized machine learning approaches for MRI-based diagnosis raise privacy concerns due to the sensitivity of medical data. This study proposes a federated learning (FL) framework for privacy-preserving AD detection and evaluates four FL algorithms—FedAvg, FedProx, FedSGD, and FedBN—using standard performance metrics. Experiments on distributed MRI datasets show that FedProx performs best (accuracy 0.806, AUC 0.869) and is most robust under non-IID conditions. FedSGD and FedAvg achieve competitive but slightly lower results, while FedBN underperforms (accuracy 0.674, AUC 0.743) due to challenges with heterogeneous batch normalization. The findings highlight FL's potential as a scalable and secure approach for multi-institutional Alzheimer's diagnosis.

abstract_ts: With the “dual-carbon” goals and the growing penetration of renewable energy, distribution networks face insufficient supply capacity under multi-scenario and multi-source loads. Traditional measures such as transformer expansion, network reinforcement, and under-frequency load shedding are costly, time-consuming, and inflexible. To overcome these limitations, this paper introduces a mobile energy storage system (MESS) and proposes an optimization scheduling method aimed at minimizing load shedding losses. The model considers charging/discharging characteristics, state of charge (SOC), traffic constraints, and network operation to coordinate multiple distributed resources. Simulations on the modified IEEE 33-bus system show that MESS can rapidly relocate to critical nodes during disasters, keeping critical load recovery above 95% and ordinary loads around 90%, while increasing total supply power from 2.3 MW to over 3.3 MW. The results highlight the significant advantages of MESS in enhancing supply capacity and resilience of distribution networks.

abstract_ts: In active distribution networks, T-connected inverter-interfaced distributed generators (IIDGs), governed by low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) control strategies, exhibit fault characteristics distinct from traditional synchronous generators. This paper establishes a distribution network model with T-connected IIDGs by incorporating IIDG control strategies and fault types. It analyzes the sequence current characteristics during short-circuit faults in active distribution lines with T-connected IIDGs. Simulation verification is conducted on the sequence component characteristics of fault currents under varying penetration levels and fault locations. The study provides a foundation for protection schemes in active distribution networks.

abstract_ts: Conventional silicon-on-insulator (SOI) devices rely on BOX and top silicon to withstand vertical voltage, limiting their breakdown voltage (BV) and thus restricting high-voltage integrated circuit (HVIC) applications for driving SiC metal-oxide-semiconductor field effect transistors (MOSFETs). This work proposes an SOI N-channel lateral diffused metal oxide semi-conductor (NLDMOS) withstanding vertical <tex>$\boldsymbol{BV}$</tex> by the substrate through high-energy ion implantation and tungsten-filled deep trench isolation (DTI). This structure achieves a <tex>$\boldsymbol{BV}$</tex> of 1600V at off-state and 1420V at <tex>$\boldsymbol{V}_{\mathbf{GS}}=15\mathbf{V}$</tex>, highly qualified for 1200V HVIC application.

abstract_ts: A wideband, high-linearity input buffer based on a class-AB Enhanced Super Source Follower (ESSF) structure is proposed in this paper. The buffer employs an AC-coupled flipped voltage follower (FVF) and ESSF dual-loop structure, enabling class-AB operation under AC coupling, achieving high bandwidth and improved linearity. Additionally, a common-mode feedback (CMFB) circuit is implemented to set the input and output common-mode voltages, ensuring compatibility with subsequent ADC stages and enhancing the robustness of the circuit. Designed in a 28-nm CMOS process, post-layout simulation shows that the proposed buffer achieves an SNDR of 58.8 dB and an SFDR of 63.6 dB at an input frequency of 15.53 GHz, with a power consumption of 47.2 mW and a 0.5-pF load.

abstract_ts: Pain is an uncomfortable experience that can significantly affect quality of life and requires an accurate assessment for appropriate treatment. However, current pain evaluation methods primarily rely on self-reporting, but these approaches are limited or inaccurate in patients with impaired communication abilities. Therefore, this study focuses on an objective pain assessment method by analyzing the physiological signal. This work proposes a machine learning (ML) framework to classify patients with myopathy and low back pain (LBP) from healthy individuals based on electromyography (EMG) signals. Statistical, Hjorth parameters, and Entropy-based features are extracted to classify these three pain categories. The results show that SVM provides the most reliable classification performance with 95% accuracy in cross-validation, while KNN and Naive Bayes perform comparatively lower with 93.75% accuracy. The superior performance of SVM can be attributed to its ability to handle nonlinear boundaries and high-dimensional feature spaces effectively. These findings highlight SVM’s potential for reliable and objective pain classification using EMG signals.

abstract_ts: Due to presence of non-linear loads in a power system network, certain current and voltage signal distortions occur, among which a particular kind is known as power system harmonics. Harmonics are defined as sinusoidal currents or voltages at frequencies that are integer multiples of the fundamental frequency. When a non-linear load is placed in a network, the signals in the same network get distorted which is due to occurrence of harmonics. In order to mitigate the distortion caused by harmonics, a model is proposed in this paper. The model includes steps like the detection of harmonics, generation of reference signal and injection of the generated waveform at the point of common coupling. An attempt has been made to design a harmonic filter using a Shunt Active Power Filter based model which may help in reducing the effect of harmonics. Instantaneous Reactive Power Theory is the key theory included in the design of the model. The efficacy of the model is indicated by the reduction of the Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) values which paves the way for practical implementation in utilities along with provision for better woring and operational benefits.

abstract_ts: The traditional relational cloud databases are struggling with scalability and performance as various types of big data grows exponentially. The NoSQL database systems are excellent choice for high availability, horizontal scalability, and semi-structured and unstructured data storage systems However, their denormalized architecture often has issues with data redundancy, increasing storage costs, network overhead, and energy usage. This paper presents a scalable data deduplication technique for NoSQL cloud storage systems that uses SHA-256 hashing with Hadoop MapReduce over HDFS for duplicate lookup and removal. The framework supports MongoDB, Cassandra, and CouchDB for cross-platform use. The experimental results on 10 GB heterogeneous log datasets demonstrate a storage saving of up to 55% with slight additional processing cost. The framework is analysed using processing time, throughput, deduplication ratio, and energy savings. The proposed technique improves data storage efficiency, bandwidth usage, and sustainability and making it suitable for modern distributed storage systems.

abstract_ts: Traditional fault diagnosis methods can not meet the demand because of the increase of equipment complexity and the multi-scale characteristics of early fault signals. Intelligent substation can collect multi-source data, which provides a basis for multi-scale feature extraction and fusion, but how to effectively integrate feature information to build a robust model is still a key issue. The overall framework designed in this study includes three-level feature processing flow. Firstly, the time domain, frequency domain and time frequency domain features of signals are extracted in parallel, and the time-frequency domain uses continuous wavelet transform to generate time-frequency maps. Then, Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) are used to mine the high-level correlation between features. Lightweight CNNMobinetTV 3 is used to extract the space-frequency features in time-frequency diagram processing, and the time-domain and frequency-domain features are input into Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) to capture the time-series dependency. Finally, the channel attention mechanism is introduced to realize dynamic weighted fusion of multi-scale features. The experiment is based on the operation data of a smart substation in State Grid from 2020 to 2023, covering the multi-category fault prediction tasks of two key equipments, namely transformers and circuit breakers. The results show that the accuracy rate of this method in transformer partial discharge fault prediction is 93.5%, the recall rate is 91.8%, the F1 value is 92.4%, and the AUC is as high as 95.7%, which is significantly better than the existing methods, showing stronger classification ability and discrimination accuracy. The ablation experiment verifies the effectiveness of each component, and the feature visualization analysis also shows that the fused feature space is more discriminating and can effectively improve the fault prediction performance.

abstract_ts: This paper presents the design and analysis of a four-port Multiple Input Multiple Output (MIMO) antenna optimized for terahertz (THz) communication systems. The proposed antenna employs a grid-based circular ring patch structure, fabricated using a high-performance Rogers dielectric substrate. Each radiating element features a circular ring with symmetrical slotting, fed via microstrip line feeding, to achieve wideband characteristics and enhanced radiation performance. The single-element antenna is extended to a compact four-port MIMO configuration by placing the elements orthogonally to ensure polarization and spatial diversity. Key MIMO performance parameters, including Envelope Correlation Coefficient (ECC), Mean Effective Gain (MEG), Sparameters, and antenna gain, have been rigorously evaluated. The proposed design achieves a peak gain of 5.3 dB, a fractional bandwidth of 10%, and an isolation level of 31 dB between ports, demonstrating strong mutual decoupling and robust performance suitable for THz applications. These results underscore the antenna's potential as a viable candidate for future high-speed, high-frequency wireless communication systems.

abstract_ts: In today’s world advanced digital devices are powered by Artificial Intelligence (AI), using these devices, we can enhance the safety, independence, and ease of navigation for Visually Impaired Persons (VIP). Many existing devices fail to provide real-time object detection, environmental feedback, and a user-friendly interface for effective assistance. The proposed work focuses on developing an AI-powered smart assistance system by integrating Deep Learning (DL) algorithms, Raspberry Pi and a camera for real-time object detection and context awareness. This allows VIPs to identify objects and avoid obstacles by providing audio cues. In addition to this main feature of the proposed system, an additional security layer is provided through a web-based application designed specifically for guardians. Guardian can easily monitor the VIPs through detecting real-time location and live camera feed, ensuring constant awareness of the VIP’s surroundings. In proposed system VIPs can switch between various modes such as indoor navigation, outdoor guidance, and text reading. Here simple hand gestures are used and detected by different sensors, making the device user-friendly and adaptable to multiple scenarios. For printed text recognition the system uses Tesseract OCR, while for object detection and obstacle recognition it employs pre-trained deep learning models for accuracy in different environments. The proposed work transforms VIPs accessibility and safety and provides real-time support by using various advanced technologies, which will help to improve quality of life for VIPs.

abstract_ts: This study examines the operational and maintenance failures that hit Boeing hard between 2022 and 2024, causing major production delays, significant financial losses, and damage to its reputation. The main reasons were safety violations, production limits imposed by the FAA, supplier defects, and labor disputes. To address these ongoing issues, this study compares Boeing’s situation with that of GE Aviation, which is recognized for its robust predictive and preventive maintenance practices. The study suggests practical solutions, such as installing IoT sensors on key production tools, utilizing edge computing to monitor processes in real-time, applying machine learning to identify recurring defects early, and automating maintenance based on the performance of these tools. It also recommends establishing centralized data systems that connect factories and suppliers to improve overall reliability. Finally, the study highlights that for Boeing to recover, it needs to move away from merely reacting to problems and instead focus on predicting and preventing them, backed by improved digital integration and enhanced quality control.

abstract_ts: Stunting remains a major public health challenge in Indonesia, with a prevalence of 21.5% in 2023, far above the national target of 14%. This study aims to enhance early stunting detection by developing a predictive model using ensemble learning methods on anthropometric data from the Kalijambe Health Center. Using a dataset of 3,129 records, we evaluated five machine learning models Logistic Regression, KNN, SVM, Random Forest, and XGBoost under various data handling scenarios. The analysis confirmed that key anthropometric measurements were the most powerful predictors of stunting. Results demonstrated that the XGBoost algorithm, particularly when combined with techniques to handle imbalanced data, achieved superior performance with 95.9% accuracy and a 0.855 F1-Score, outperforming traditional models. These findings highlight the practical potential of integrating the XGBoost model into existing health information systems to enable earlier and more accurate identification of at-risk children, thereby supporting more effective nutritional intervention strategies.

abstract_ts: Advancements in information technology have significantly enhanced efficiency and accessibility in public services through the implementation of various digital applications within government institutions. However, the large number of systems deployed across agencies often leads to misalignment and functional redundancy. These issues can be addressed by developing a structured information system that supports operational continuity, improves efficiency, and accelerates responses to technical issues. This study aims to design a web-based helpdesk information system to handle technical problems occurring in the internal applications of the Directorate of Road and Bridge Engineering. The system was developed using the Waterfall model and implemented with technologies such as MySQL, PHP, JavaScript, HTML/CSS, and the Laravel framework. System performance was analyzed using the PIECES framework, while functionality was validated through black-box testing to ensure feature compliance with user requirements. The results indicate that the proposed helpdesk system enables real-time reporting and monitoring of technical issues, thereby improving efficiency, transparency, and report management. The implementation of this system is expected to enhance service quality at the Directorate of Road and Bridge Engineering through faster and more organized responses to technical problems.

abstract_ts: The accelerating transformation of business environments compels firms to continuously adapt to maintain long-term sustainability. This study presents a comparative analysis of three classical machine learning methods: Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Random Forest for classifying firm adaptability and sustainability levels. The dataset consists of key organizational indicators (X, Y, Z) that represent strategic, operational, and environmental dimensions of firm performance. Each model was trained and evaluated using multiple feature combinations (XY, YZ, XZ, and XYZ) to assess the influence of feature integration on classification accuracy and generalization capability. Experimental results demonstrate that the Random Forest model consistently outperforms Logistic Regression and SVM, achieving the highest accuracy of 82% and balanced precision-recall metrics when utilizing the full feature set (XYZ). In contrast, Logistic Regression and SVM show limited generalization with accuracies below 52%, indicating their reduced effectiveness in multidimensional classification tasks. These findings highlight the superiority of ensemble-based approaches in modeling complex interdependencies among organizational factors. The study contributes to advancing data-driven methods for evaluating corporate adaptability and sustainability within dynamic business environments.

abstract_ts: The potential risks associated with cyber-threats posed by IoT devices are significant and diverse, e.g., endangering users, causing asset losses, to damaging the physical ecosystem. This research aims to develop an appropriate Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) to protect IoT networks in MSMEs, which have limited budgets for network security, and invest in their employees’ security knowledge in security is nearly impossible. At the same time, the risk imposed by cyber-attacks is as high as larger companies. By integrating Raspberry Pi with SnortML in Snort, this is expected to be an alternative solution for MSMEs to detect and prevent cybercrime, especially DDoS and exploit attacks from reconnaissance to exfiltration. The research method includes implementing Snort software and employing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) to enhance detection accuracy on Raspberry Pi, such as Raspberry Pi 3B and Raspberry Pi 4; on both devices, we then analyze resource utilization during the attack experiments and evaluate the model’s metrics. Evaluations are conducted using confusion matrix metrics (accuracy, precision, and recall), along with resource utilization assessments (CPU and RAM). The model LSTM and CNN on Raspberry Pi 3B have an accuracy of 60.39% and 59.08%, while in Raspberry Pi 4, the accuracy is 61.61% and 65.86%. This paper chose CNN with Raspberry Pi 4 as the best model because it resulted in an accuracy of 65.86%.

abstract_ts: In this rapid technological advancement era, MSMEs needs a strategy to adapt the digitalization pace. One such adaptation is the usage of digital accounting in managing MSMEs’ financial data to fulfill their needs. By integrate the DeLone McLean success model and UTAUT 3, this research evaluates how digital accounting is utilized in managing financial data for MSMEs, using a quantitative approach through structured questionnaires for data collection. Data analysis is performed using the PLS-SEM technique, with Smart PLS serving as the analytical tools. Based on this research’s findings, it indicates that user satisfaction and user’s use play an important role in driving net benefit. Personal innovativeness and system quality are found to significantly influence use. System quality and use has an impact on user satisfaction. Habit and personal innovativeness have an impact to behavioral intention. MSMEs and digital accounting developers can use these findings as consideration to develop digital accounting in the future and improve the digital accounting system.

abstract_ts: This paper's objective is to deliver a state-of-the-art survey in Internet of Things-based systems for monitoring air quality. This survey offers insights for the current platforms, communication protocols, smart sensors, microcontrollers, and system architectures. First, we examine the field from different perspectives that we identify after a thorough review of the literature, namely architectures, enabling technologies and common protocols, low-cost air quality sensors, and data management. Then, we review the aforementioned perspectives and we propose a new system design for air quality monitoring.

abstract_ts: The integrated joint module is a highly integrated mechatronic actuator and serves as a core component in both humanoid robots and quadruped robots. To provide precise position feedback, the joint module employs high-precision dual encoders. However, factors such as installation errors, electromagnetic interference, and violent movements can interfere with them, even causing joint loss of control in severe cases. This paper presents a novel approach that integrates dual encoders with a sliding mode observer. The observer is designed to provide real-time estimation of the motor rotor position and to instantaneously substitute abnormal encoder readings with observer values. This approach establishes a robust safety redundancy mechanism, effectively addressing the issue of abrupt encoder failures. The efficacy and feasibility of the proposed method are validated through experimental investigations. The outcomes of this research hold significant promise for enhancing the control reliability of joint modules.

abstract_ts: This paper aims to connect the learning design process with managing the risk of student failure in technologyenhanced learning scenarios. Grounded in the field of Learning Design, which explores the interplay of pedagogy, content, and technology, the study highlights how these components shape student engagement and the overall effectiveness and quality of a learning scenario, as well as feed into a risk analysis process. Using methodological elements from the Grey Wolf Optimizer algorithm, the research optimizes key design parameters to support risk-oriented design decisions. The findings result in the development of a learning design framework to proactively manage the risk of student failure. This work contributes to existing literature by integrating risk management into the learning design process.

abstract_ts: Automatic Text Summarization (ATS) of Greek legal documents is a challenging task that combines the complexities of legal language with the linguistic intricacies of Greek. This paper presents a structured review of ATS methods applied to this domain. We compare extractive, abstractive, and hybrid techniques, highlighting their strengths and limitations. Emphasis is placed on the structural and morphological challenges of Greek legal texts. We also catalog and compare all publicly available datasets relevant to the task and examine existing summarization approaches, including graph-based algorithms and transformer-based models. The paper consolidates current efforts, identifies key limitations, and outlines directions for future research in legal NLP for lowresource languages.

abstract_ts: Digital Twins are an innovative approach to medical education, allowing realistic simulation of clinical procedures. Their combination with feedback technologies, such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), significantly enhances training in complex and specialized scenarios, such as those related to neurodegenerative diseases. In this paper, we present an integrated virtual avatar system with embedded AI, designed based on data from real patients in everyday environments. This educational environment offers an immersive and interactive learning experience, targeting both healthcare professionals, caregivers and ordinary users, through questions and scenarios that simulate reality. The methodology is based on machine learning models and real-time behavioral simulations, aiming to reproduce symptoms of conditions such as Parkinson's and Alzheimer's disease. The system allows researchers to practice diagnostic and treatment strategies in a controlled environment, while providing caregivers with practical guidelines for daily patient care. In addition, integration into virtual reality (VR) environments is supported. A user study was conducted to evaluate the effectiveness of the system, focusing on user engagement, educational value and clinical applicability. Results demonstrate significant improvement in knowledge acquisition and retention, as well as in the development of diagnostic skills.

abstract_ts: The commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) equipment use in military applications is increasing steadily. However, there is a need for evaluation of the COTS equipment to determine whether it complies with the military electromagnetic environmental effects. In this paper, the compliance of COTS equipment in a military environment is assessed from the external RF transmitter effect point of view. The RF susceptibility test methods in MIL-STD-461G RS103 and IEC61000-4-3 are compared. The test setups were modelled and simulated under the same conditions. The simulation results obtained show that the IEC61000-4-3 test setup detects susceptibility at lower electric fields compared to the RS103 test setup.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a 138-to-170GHz on-chip coupler designed for dielectric waveguide (DWG) interconnections, enabling high-frequency signal transmission for next-generation millimeter-wave systems. The proposed structure couples the signal from a wedge-shaped substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW) into a DWG to provide an efficient chip-to-chip transmission path, supporting wideband signal propagation in the D-band. Fabricated in 65-nm CMOS process, the coupler achieves a 32.1 GHz 3-dB bandwidth from 137.9 to 170 GHz with a maximum in-band ripple of 2.9 dB. The functionality and performance of the on-chip coupler are validated through experimental results, demonstrating its suitability for D-band transceiver systems in high-speed communication, radar sensing, and chip interconnect applications.

abstract_ts: Localized thermal hotspots in cryogenic CMOS systems require distributed temperature sensing with minimal power and area overhead. abstract_ts: Advanced microprocessor designs enhance memory-level parallelism through large-scale scheduling resources. However, fixed-configuration scheduling resources induce significant energy overhead. To address this challenge, this study presents a collaborative optimization approach spanning microarchitecture and circuit levels, integrating a resource bottleneck dynamic scaling strategy with an adaptive Load-Store Queue. The proposed scheme dynamically adjusts the scale of scheduling resources by monitoring performance bottlenecks induced by scheduling resources during program execution. Experimental results demonstrate a 27.9% energy reduction across SPEC CPU2017 benchmarks and a 35.6% reduction in Graph Algorithm Platform Benchmark Suite.

abstract_ts: This study investigates the control of a pendulum system with a single angular degree of freedom, using the Sliding Mode Control (SMC) method. The system dynamics are obtained by applying the Euler-Lagrange approach and formulated as a nonlinear second-order differential equation. Four different sliding surface structures have been designed: classical SMC, PID-based, PI-PD-based and FOPDT-based SMC. Each control law is formulated individually. The controllers were developed using MATLAB/Simulink, with optimal gain parameters identified through the fmincon algorithm to reduce the integral of squared error (ISE). The simulation results offer a comparative examination of the transient responses associated with each type of sliding surface. The study’s results show that the most effective structure results in the lowest overshoot, the shortest settling time and smallest ISE value. This research introduces an effective and optimized method for controlling pendulum systems with the application of SMC techniques in various sliding surface configurations. A FOPDT-based SMC structure achieved the lowest overshoot, the shortest settling time, and minimal ISE, thus proving its superior performance for pendulum control.

abstract_ts: True random number generators (TRNGs) play a crucial role in hardware security, which can provide unpredictable randomness and strengthen system security. In this paper, a TRNG with dual-entropy sources is proposed based on metastability of DFF & MUX and jitter of RO, which can complement each other. TAF-DPS clock generators are adopted to generate multichannel time-varying signals, which themselves contain randomness. The final output was verified by using the NIST randomness test suite. The circuit design is implemented on FPGA. Moreover, the proposed TRNG achieves a random sequence generation rate of 250 Mbps and shows strong robustness against temperature variation. Our findings may have the potential to improve the security of hardware devices and systems.

abstract_ts: Wireless power transfer (WPT) to implanted devices is an elegant solution that can obviate the need for batteries. As more functions are implemented in the implanted devices, more than one output voltages are typically requested for the optimal performance. In this talk, first, we summarized several representative designs of the regulating rectifier with multiple outputs. Being aware of the shortcomings of previous works, we proposed a design that can support the simultaneous charging of multiple outputs in a half cycle. Experimental results verified a peak power conversion efficiency of 92.2 % and output power of 131 mW. These performance indexes are the best compared to previous works.

abstract_ts: This paper presents an FPGA-accelerated architecture for real-time Scale-Invariant Feature Transform (SIFT) algorithm implementation. To address the high computational complexity of the algorithm, we design a pyramid structure with reduced layers but maintained high feature point generation rate, implement a threshold-based extreme point detection circuit, replace division operations with multiply-add units in descriptor normalization, and realize a fully pipelined circuit architecture. Experimental results on the Xilinx Kintex-7 XC7K325T platform demonstrate that our design achieves a processing speed of 3.19ms/frame for 640×480 images, reaching a 115.4× speedup over CPU implementation while preserving feature matching accuracy.

abstract_ts: Flash-based solid state drives (SSDs) incorporate DRAM for data cache, which significantly optimize performance. Due to the volatile nature of DARM, the essential metadata information and address mapping table are easy to be lost, hence it is difficult to guarantee the reliability of the information. One promising solution is replacing DRAM with Spin-transfer Torque Magnetoresistive Random Access Memory (STT-MRAM) which is a non-volatile memory with low latency. This paper proposed a SSD controller architecture of employing STT-MRAM and DRAM residing on the DDR4 bus. The DDR4 controller contained in the SSD controller was optimized for support handling differing timing, command and addressing requirements for STT-MRAM. Furthermore, a system level error correcting code (ECC) was applied to the STT-MRAM interface to correct bit errors occurring inside the STT-MRAM chip. This SSD controller was silicon-proven working well, and presented the expected read & write speed of STT-MRAM.

abstract_ts: Over the past decade, AI-driven algorithms have significantly improved the reliability of object detection in both traditional computer vision tasks and Earth Observation (EO) applications. With the rapid increase in EO satellite deployments and growing constraints on communication bandwidth, there is a critical need for on-board data processing and selection. Tailoring Deep Learning (DL) models to specific mission objectives enables prioritization of relevant data, thereby reducing transmission requirements. However, deploying efficient DL models on-board satellites presents multiple challenges due to constraints on memory, computation, energy, and limited uplink bandwidth for updates.To address these limitations, AGENIUM Space (AGS) has developed a software solution for simplifying Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), currently applied in a proof-of-concept for plastic detections in oceans. This effort is part of the Edge SpAIce project, a collaboration involving AGS, CERN, the National Technical University of Athens (NTUA), and Endurosat (EDS). NTUA is responsible for developing accurate DNNs and corresponding datasets; AGS focuses on model compression for on-board deployment; CERN provides the hls4ml toolchain for efficient implementation on SoC-FPGAs; and EDS contributes multispectral imagery from their Copernicus Complementary Mission satellite BALKAN-01 (launched January 2025) and leads the in-orbit validation of the models.Building on its prior experience with the CORTEX and DEEPCUBE ESA’s projects, AGS has extended its neural network simplification tools in Edge SpAIce. Enhancements include intermediate feature distillation and pruning mechanisms to complement or replace distillation. These methods will be evaluated on both conventional computer vision and EO datasets for tasks such as semantic segmentation.This paper aims to detail possible methodologies for reducing DNN depending on the model architecture, the database and the target objects to be detected while maintaining high performance.

abstract_ts: The prediction of startup success is a significant process within the entrepreneur ecosystem. It provides information for equity funding decisions, incubator support, and policy decisions. In this paper, we have introduced a machine learning method for predicting startup success using features associated with the funding history of the startup, team attributes, and location-based features. The dataset contains a wealth of startup records, each labeled as successful or failed, with features created from financial and operational metrics. After preprocessing the dataset, we trained and tested three different machine learning models: Random Forest, AdaBoost, and XGBoost. Then, we built a hybrid ensemble model with these base learners and optimized the hybrid model through cross-validation and tuning. The ensemble model produced better performance than individual classifiers, predicted startups’ outcomes with accuracy, with a majority beating 90% in prediction success. The results demonstrate that machine learning is potentially a useful approach for predicting the outcome of startup ventures, a useful resource in informing investment decisions, strategies, and support systems for entrepreneurs.

abstract_ts: The fast development of Electric Motor Vehicles (EMVs) is making unprecedented hardware smart integrated electronic systems, networked systems and digital connectivity. EMVs equipped with advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS), over-the-air firmware updates, and Internet of Things (IoT) interfaces are now exposed to cyber threats in vehicle networks like the CAN bus 1. Conventional rule-based security systems are ineffective in dealing with intelligent dynamic threats such as those that threaten the security of the driver or the integrity of the data. This paper studies the use of AI-assisted security techniques - namely real-time intrusion prevention systems (IPS) based on deep learning models - to strengthen EMVs' defensive mechanisms against newer cyber-attacks. We Introduce a RNN-based and CNN-based and networks like CNN-LSTM to detect and stop the misuse of the CAN bus in real time network for detection and prevention of rogue behavior on CAN bus. Our methodology encompasses gathering and cleaning vehicle CAN Datasets, discovering temporal and spatial pattern of cyber anomalies, and model building for distinguishing between normal and cyber-invasive traffic. The system performance is tested on both real and artificial data to verify the suitability of our models against various evolving threats, including spoofing, DoS, and message injection. The use of deep learning-based IPS has several advantages over conventional approaches. These advantages are adaptive learning with new threat patterns, low false positive rate and real-time decision making with low latency. Moreover, the use of light-weight AI models running on embedded systems in the vehicle avoids both excessive impact on the performance and power consumption—key elements for EMV systems. The work not only adds a scalable mechanism to meet the increasing cybersecurity demand in modern electric vehicles, but also leads to potential integrations with vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communications and autonomous driving systems in the future. The research establishes a foundation to build trust in ITS (intelligent transportation system) by securing the cybersecurity risks of in-vehicle networks using AI. The findings demonstrate that the proposed hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in terms of both classification accuracy, the speed of detection and generalizability to unseen types of attack vectors, and hence confirm the efficacy of the proposed real-time deep learning IPS architecture for EMVs.

abstract_ts: This study proposes a Ciphertext-Policy Attribute-Based Encryption (CP-ABE) architecture to solve important cloud security concerns, including the problem of unauthorized data access. The flexibility needed in dynamic cloud settings is sometimes lacking in traditional access control systems. By directly integrating access policies into the ciphertext, CP-ABE provides a scalable and fine-grained approach that enables data owners to impose exact permission depending on user characteristics. The proposed system outlines the procedures for key creation, encryption, and controlled access, as well as the responsibilities of the Data Owner, Cloud Service Provider, and Data Users. The system, which is implemented with the Charm-Crypto module, uses Boolean expressions to establish access controls and CSV files to handle user characteristics. A thorough security study shows that, even in the face of illegal access attempts and data manipulation, the system is successful in maintaining data confidentiality, integrity, and access control. The outcomes verify that the CP-ABE technique is both resilient and practically feasible in actual cloud systems. In the future, decentralized key management and dynamic attribute update support will be investigated to further improve system robustness and flexibility.

abstract_ts: Stock price prediction is a very complex yet important task in finance which we attribute to the intrinsic volatility and non-linearity of time series. In this work we present a deep learning approach for forecasting Power Grid Corporation of India’s stocks using Bidirectional Long Term Short Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks in which we have put in Key Technical indicators. The model we present includes history close prices and also what we term as momentum and volatility based features which out in to include the Relative Strength Index (RSI), Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) and Bollinger Bands. Also we did data normalization and preprocessing which we followed by conversion of the data to time sequences in preparation for the BiLSTM model which in turn is able to identify past and future temporal relationships. The model is validated by visually inspecting and also statistically using measures such as Mean Squared Error (MSE), at a prediction rate of approximately 92.4% on test data. Experimental findings indicate that the use of technical indicators significantly improves the predictability and explainability of the model. This research contributes to the present body of literature on financial time series forecasting and learns from deploying cutting-edge neural architectures in the Indian energy industry.

abstract_ts: Anemia affects more than 1.6 billion people worldwide and remains a critical global health concern due to its impact on oxygen delivery and patient well-being. Conventional diagnostic methods such as complete blood count (CBC) and peripheral blood smear (PBS) analysis, while reliable, are labor-intensive, resource-dependent, and impractical for large-scale screening in low-resource settings. Recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI) and deep learning (DL) provide promising opportunities for automated, accurate, and scalable anemia detection. Unlike prior works that focus solely on reviewing techniques or applying traditional ML models with handcrafted features, this paper makes a dual contribution: (1) it presents a systematic review of machine learning and deep learning approaches for anemia detection, highlighting the evolution from conventional classifiers to CNNs, Vision Transformers, and hybrid multimodal frameworks; and (2) it introduces and empirically validates a fine-tuned ResNet50 model applied to PBS images. The proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance with 98% accuracy and a 99.1% F1-score, outperforming several existing methods. In addition, this work identifies key challenges—including dataset scarcity, generalization across diverse imaging conditions, and model interpretability—and outlines future directions such as multimodal fusion, explainable AI, and clinical integration. Together, these contributions advance the development of intelligent and clinically viable anemia detection systems for improved healthcare outcomes.

abstract_ts: The objective of the Vehicle Routing Problem with Time Windows (VRPTW) is to plan a set of delivery routes for vehicles, satisfying the needs of several customers with time windows, while minimizing the total length of the routes. This paper propose the POMO-TW model by integrating a specialized Mask mechanism (MTW) with the POMO framework to handle VRPTW constraints. To address scale limitations, we develop the Continual Learning-based Route Optimization Model (CL-ROM) via a continual learning-based generalization scheme. Experiments on 20-, 50-, and 100-node instances show POMO-TW outperforms advanced algorithms, achieving a 1.16% gap from Gurobi’s optimal solution on 20-node problems. CL-ROM maintains high quality across scales, approaching single-scale POMO-TW performance on smaller instances and exceeding it on 100-node problems, validating the generalization strategy.

abstract_ts: This paper tackles the challenge of detecting early-stage fires in industrial workshops, a setting complicated by faint flames, highly transparent smoke, and complex background interference. To address these issues, we optimize the YOLOv8 model for both computational efficiency and detection accuracy. We propose the MMD model, an enhanced architecture that integrates a lightweight MobileNetV3 backbone, the MLCA multi-dimensional attention mechanism, and a Dual-Conv module. These modifications improve the model's feature extraction capabilities for flame and smoke while maintaining a high inference speed. Experimental results demonstrate that the MMD model achieves an mAP@0.5 of 89.4%, a 1.1% improvement over the baseline YOLOv8. The performance gain is especially significant in smoke detection, where the mAP@0.5 increases from 91.4% to 94.1% (+2.7%). Furthermore, the model operates at 278 FPS, making it suitable for real-time applications. This research presents a robust and efficient method for the precise, real-time detection of incipient fires in demanding industrial environments.

abstract_ts: Accurate weed identification is critical for ensuring the reliability of precision spraying systems in maize fields. Although deep learning has advanced weed detection, most existing methods pay limited attention to the growth stages of maize and weeds, leading to misclassification when early-stage maize resembles broadleaf weeds in appearance. To address this challenge, we propose YOLO-GSA, a growth-stage-aware detector trained on the WeedMaize dataset. The model integrates an MS-Block into the backbone to enhance multi-scale feature representation and incorporates C2f Snake into the neck to improve the extraction of fine-grained structural details. Experimental results show that YOLO-GSA improves mAP@0.5 by 4 percentage points compared with the original YOLOv8, demonstrating its effectiveness for weed detection in maize fields.

abstract_ts: The rising cases of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus make the need to develop early prediction mechanisms that are highly accurate and those that are clinically interpretable. This paper critically compares six machine-learning models, Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine, K-Nearest Neighbours, Random Forest, Gradient-Boosted Decision Tree and XGBoost, which are trained on PIMA Indians Diabetes Dataset. The Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) and severe data preprocessing are used in our methodology to reduce intrinsic class imbalance. The ensemble models are the best since they achieve an Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (ROC-AUC) of 0.82. Random Forest has the best Average Precision of 0.71 on this imbalanced clinical data. To go beyond predictive performance, we use the SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) model of interpretability. SHAP analysis proves that the model is driven by clinically salient variables, which are mostly Glucose, then BMI, and Age. The high performance and interpretability together, therefore, make our model a viable addition to the clinical decision-support systems.

abstract_ts: In this paper, we have discussed about a multilingual Text-to-Speech (TTS) system for low-resource Indian languages, like Bhojpuri, Magahi, and Awadhi, using the Cross-lingual Text-to-Speech (XTTS) model. We address the issue of linguistic diversity and digital resource scarcity. We review existing approaches, inspect phonological problems, and present Multilingual Text-to-Speech. Our purpose is to offer a framework for scalable, low-resource TTS systems for Indo-Aryan languages. Although spoken by millions in northern and eastern Bihar, these Indo-Aryan languages have insufficient digital speech synthesis resources because of a lack of enough annotated corpora and imbalanced linguistic documentation. We tried to solve these issues with the help of XTTS's cross-lingual ability and few-shot learning processes, along with transfer from Hindi as the source language. Our approach takes a two-step process: (1) cross-lingual mapping of Hindi phonemes to target languages, (2) multilingual fine-tuning across language-specific embedding.

abstract_ts: This research presents a novel counterfactual generation framework tailored for high-stakes medical image analysis, leveraging Denoising Autoencoders (DAE) to synthesize realistic, class-conditional perturbations that preserve anatomical fidelity while altering diagnostically relevant features. The proposed methodology integrates a generative latent-space manipulation pipeline with multi-scale feature disentanglement, enabling precise modulation of pathology-specific attributes without introducing non-biological artifacts. To ensure clinical interpretability, the system incorporates structural similarity preservation constraints, domain-specific priors, and adaptive noise regularization, thus producing counterfactual instances that remain consistent with medical plausibility. The approach is rigorously evaluated on benchmark radiological datasets, with quantitative assessments using F1-score, Dice similarity coefficient, and perceptual realism metrics, alongside qualitative validation from expert radiologists. This work aims to advance explainable AI in healthcare by enabling trustworthy, transparent decision support systems, bridging the gap between deep learning–driven diagnostic models and human-centric interpretability requirements in clinical environments.

abstract_ts: The success of in vitro fertilization (IVF) procedures depends on the evaluation of embryo quality. Because manual evaluation by embryologists is subjective and sensitive to interobserver variability, automated, data-driven methods are required. In this study, we suggest a deep learning-based classification system that uses convolution neural networks (CNNs) to assign “Good” or “Not Good” quality labels to embryo images taken on Days 3 and 5 of development. A stratified split is used to train and evaluate the model after the dataset had been preprocessed using augmentation and normalization approaches. Although sensitivity towards “Good” embryos have to be improved, experimental results show a promising performance in identifying “Not Good” embryos with a cumulative accuracy of $\mathbf{8 9. 4 7 \%}$. To assess model consistency, a 3-fold cross-validation was also carried out, and the results demonstrated shows consistent performance across various subsets. The results lend credence to the viability of using CNN model built with the specifications of Adam optimizer, Binary Cross Entropy of 20 epochs with the batch size of 12 for 3 fold crossfold validation to help embryologists choose viable embryos, which would enhance IVF results.

abstract_ts: There is a growing body of research on Black women faculty in institutions of higher education highlighting how Black women have thrived on college campuses, even when many institutions were not built for their success. However, there is scant evidence of the mental and physical health consequences of the racial and gender stress that Black women encounter in social contexts such as colleges and universities, including depression, anxiety, and detrimental physical health diagnoses (e.g., cancer, hypertension, and reproductive health concerns). In this integrative review of the literature, we bring together two bodies of evidence: scholarship on Black women faculty in higher education and research about mental and physical health conditions for Black women. We consider the conceptual and empirical assumptions of scholarly work about Black women faculty and Black women’s health outcomes to pave a path toward future research and theorizing that aim to help recruit and retain Black women faculty in the academy.

abstract_ts: The electro-mechanical brake (EMB), as a brake-by-wire system that generates clamping force through the torque motor and mechanical mechanism, features compact structure, fast response, and high control accuracy. However, due to the strong nonlinear characteristics of EMB and disturbances such as friction, the clamping force in EMB exhibits a hysteresis effect, making it difficult to accurately control the clamping force directly based on the motor rotation angle signal. To address this issue, a neural network learning mechanism is introduced into the clamping force estimation of EMB, and a low-cost estimation method based on convolutional neural network and bidirectional long short term (CNN-BiLSTM) is proposed. First, through the analysis of hysteresis effects, it is concluded that the EMB clamping force is related to the current motor rotation angle, historical motor rotation angles, and historical clamping force outputs. Subsequently, by integrating the bidirectional learning capability of BiLSTM with the strong feature extraction capability of CNN, the CNN and BiLSTM are combined to reduce the interference of redundant input information on prediction results, thereby effectively improving the accuracy of EMB clamping force estimation. Finally, under given motor rotation angle reversal conditions, experimental tests demonstrate that the clamping force estimation model based on CNN-BiLSTM proposed in this paper has better estimation accuracy compared with LSTM and BiLSTM.

abstract_ts: Cervical cancer is the leading cause of death for women in Indonesia. The IVA screening method widely used in primary care still faces limitations in accuracy because it relies on visual observation without assistive devices. This research developed a prototype of a digital colposcope based on the Raspberry Pi 4 to improve image quality to support early detection. The system features an autofocus 64MP ArduCam camera, intensity-controlled LED lighting, and a polarization filter to reduce reflections. Tests were carried out on image acquisition performance, light intensity, power efficiency, as well as image quality evaluation and lesion detection validation with the YOLO algorithm. The results showed a frame rate of 85.5 FPS (VGA), medically standard lighting (>1000 lux), and a minimum reflection area (0.01%). The optimal configuration is achieved at 16MP resolution, 15 cm distance and maximum illumination. Lesion detection by YOLO reaches up to 97% confidence. This prototype demonstrates the potential as an affordable and effective IVA tool, supporting clinical observation and visual documentation in primary care.

abstract_ts: This paper presents an Automated-Machine Learning (AutoML) framework for soft-failure classification in optical networks. Unlike traditional manual approaches, the proposed method automatically selects and optimizes machine learning models with minimal human intervention. Using a large dataset based on the Euro28 topology, multiple algorithms are evaluated under the AutoML pipeline and benchmark them against a baseline Deep-Neural Network (DNN). Results show that the Extra-Trees classifier model achieves the best performance (accuracy > 0.999) while reducing training time by more than 3 times compared to the DNN, demonstrating both computational efficiency and predictive superiority. Feature analysis further reveals that the received power and the ECL current are the dominant indicators of failure, providing valuable insights for network monitoring. These findings highlight AutoML as a practical and scalable solution for failure management in future high-capacity optical networks.

abstract_ts: Accurate segmentation of anatomical structures in fetal cardiac ultrasound views is crucial for the diagnosis of congenital heart disease (CHD) in fetuses. However, this task remains highly challenging due to the small size and complex morphology of the fetal heart, as well as the presence of high noise and blurry boundaries inherent in ultrasound imaging. Existing methods typically rely on precise annotations provided by obstetric experts, which are both time-consuming and costly. To address these challenges, we propose CSP-SAM, a novel SAM-based segmentation framework specifically designed for fetal cardiac ultrasound image analysis. Specifically, we design a CNN adapter branch to enhance the spatial representation capability of the image encoder and mask decoder, enabling the model to better capture the complex anatomical features of the fetal heart. To reduce reliance on expert annotations, we propose a self-prompting strategy that autonomously generates high-quality point, box, and mask prompts for effective guidance in the segmentation process. By integrating these components into the CSP-SAM framework, our method is extensively evaluated on two public fetal cardiac ultrasound datasets and one private clinical dataset, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance compared to existing models.

abstract_ts: The Green and Digital Transition is a significant shift in society, requiring equitable strategies and anticipating socio-economic implications. This chapter advocates for relational urban living, integrating communal existence, regenerative knowledge, and digital tools. The Quintuple Helix Innovation model is used to define the Twin Transition policy, emphasizing digital technology in metropolitan construction and the growing role of intangible components like knowledge, local culture, and labour. The Twin Transition concept involves place-based solutions, sustainability, networking opportunities, and technology transfer. The research aims to develop guidelines for sustainable habitats in vulnerable territories in metropolitan regions, focusing on data-driven innovation and strategic partnerships. An ‘E-Gateway for digitisation in the metropolitan economy’ strategy is proposed to enhance economic potential in Italian urban systems, including digital infrastructure development, skills enhancement, supportive policies, and collaborative partnerships. E-Global Gateways can facilitate global economic integration, enhancing local digital economy participation, and promoting cultural exchange and understanding between countries.

abstract_ts: The study of classification methods for class imbalanced medical images not only helps to improve the performance of medical image processing methods and the accuracy of medical diagnosis, but also has important significance for promoting medical research and improving medical standards. Aiming at the problem of how to correct the bias between the majority class and the minority class in the method, this paper proposes a class imbalanced medical image semi-supervised classification framework BCMatch based on bias correction. First, to address the problem of low utilization of unlabeled data for minority classes under semi-supervised learning, This paper proposes a dynamic pseudo-label threshold adjustment module called LTCPL based on the idea of curriculum learning. It enables adaptive adjustment of pseudo-label thresholds for each category, facilitating better use of unlabeled data and increasing attention to minority classes, thereby correcting the encoder’s bias caused by class imbalance. In addition, to address the classifier’s bias of predicting all data as majority classes, this paper proposes a data selection module CSS based on feature cosine similarity, which freezes the encoder’s parameters and allows the classifier to perform two-stage training on the balanced data alone, thereby correcting the method classifier’s bias towards majority and minority classes on the decision boundary. Experimental results on the ISIC2019 Dermatology Dataset and ChestXRay Chest X-ray Dataset show that BCMatch is superior to the most advanced semi-supervised classification methods, and can further improve performance when combined with two-stage training.

abstract_ts: Urban rail power transformers are key components of the urban rail power supply system, and their fault detection is essential for ensuring the reliability of power delivery. In recent years, voiceprint-based fault detection for transformer have emerged as a promising direction due to their non-contact and non-intrusive nature. Especially, the application of deep learning techniques has led to significant progress in this field. However, challenges such as class imbalance, limited feature diversity, and noise interference still persist. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel voiceprit recognition method for urban rail power transformers based on data augmentation and multidomain fusion. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, experiments were conducted on a dataset provided by a subway power supply department. The results demonstrate that our method achieves 100 % accuracy on the test set, and maintains over 98 % diagnostic accuracy even under high noise conditions of SNR=-4dB, significantly outperforming other baseline methods.

abstract_ts: Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly reshaping military and humanitarian healthcare, from battlefield triage and evacuation to crisis mapping, logistics and predictive analytics. These tools promise greater efficiency and improved clinical outcomes but also raise acute ethical and legal risks, including opaque life-and-death decision-making, algorithmic bias, dehumanisation through autonomous systems, and the facilitation or concealment of war crimes. The chapter maps the emerging AI law and ethics landscape relevant to these settings, showing how data protection, medical device law, product liability, Human Rights Law and International Humanitarian Law only partially address AI’s systemic and autonomous features. It then examines new global and European initiatives, in particular the EU Artificial Intelligence Act and the Council of Europe AI Convention, and highlights persisting gaps around defence, dual-use systems, accountability and liability. Finally, it sketches a multi-level reform agenda—combining international law, national legislation, institutional governance and professional education—to safeguard human rights, accountability and trust in military and humanitarian healthcare.

abstract_ts: This study provides a comparative review of Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques for condition monitoring and rotor fault detection in Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs). Machine Learning (ML) has gained prominence across diverse domains, including electrical machines, for enhancing motor performance, minimizing downtime, and enabling predictive maintenance. These advancements yield significant cost savings, extend operational lifespans, and improve machine safety. Early detection of rotor faults is critical, as their occurrence adversely impacts PMSM performance. While existing studies have primarily focused on ML applications for demagnetization and eccentricity faults, research on rotor fault detection, diagnosis, and monitoring in PMSMs remains limited. Consequently, AI-based condition monitoring requires further development to achieve robust rotor fault detection and diagnosis. This paper systematically reviews traditional and AI-based methodologies employed in current research, highlighting gaps and future directions.

abstract_ts: Fibrillar collagens are the most abundant structural proteins in vertebrates, forming the backbone of connective tissues such as skin, bone, tendon, and cartilage. In bony fish (teleosts), fibrillar collagens exhibit unique genetic and biochemical properties that reflect a complex evolutionary history of this taxonomic group and its adaptation to diverse aquatic environments-from tropical to polar habitats. This review summarises the current understanding of the genetic organisation and biochemical characteristics of fibrillar collagens in bony fish. They show significant differences in amino acid composition to mammalian collagens, especially in cold-adapted species, where collagens display lower thermal stability and reduced hydroxyproline content relative to their mammalian counterparts.Advances in genomic, transcriptomic, and proteomic profiling have provided new perspectives on the molecular diversity and tissue-specific roles of collagen chains in teleosts. Furthermore, the biomedical potential of fish-derived collagens is receiving growing attention, particularly in biomaterials, wound healing, tissue engineering, and drug delivery systems, owing to their biocompatibility, low immunogenicity, and ease of extraction from byproducts of the fishing industry.By looking at molecular, structural, and applied perspectives, this review highlights the relevance of bony fish collagens as a subject of fundamental biological interest and as a valuable resource for biotechnological and biomedical innovation.

abstract_ts: The Renewable energy sources development (RES) is a main priority of the Republic of Moldova. This priority is determined by two factors: high dependence of energy import and need to decarbonize energy sector. Additionally, energy crises started in 2022 as result of Russia war in Ukraine, impacted negative energy resources price in the Republic of Moldova. As response to this energy crises the deployment of renewable energy sources for electricity production (eRES) started to increase very fast. Both investors and consumers started to install electricity generation units based on RES. Only in last two years eRES capacities overcome 600 MW, that is equal to summer demand capacity of the country. Overall installed PV capacities for commercial and own consumption purpose (prosumers) increased essentially. The exact installed by prosumers PV capacities is unknown and this can become an issue power system for stability. From other side prosumers PV capacities have mostly one-phase connection. Considering this fact and non-symmetrical load in rural communities frequently appear issues regarding power quality, including disconnections of PV installations from the power grid. The paper present case study of PV systems influences on power quality parameters in a rural area at voltage level of <tex>$\mathbf{0. 4 k V}$</tex> for different situations and present solutions based on energy communities and battery storage system how to improve power quality parameters and share in more efficient mode expenses and benefits between members of this energy community. The additional 33 % of economic benefits can be obtained then PV owners are associated in energy community. Using of battery storage can increase local consumption of PV generated electricity up to 95 %.
