abstract_ts: This paper presents an optimized slot configuration of a CPW-fed microstrip patch antenna in an attempt to enhance gain and bandwidth while preserving the resonant frequency of interest. The antenna was simulated on an FR4 substrate with the resonance frequency of interest being 2.4 GHz. The substrate was dimensioned approximately <tex>$0.32 \lambda \times 0.46 \lambda \times 0.012 \lambda$</tex>. The patch dimension was approximately <tex>$0.24 \lambda \times 0.31 \lambda$</tex>. The introduction of a slot initially shifted the resonance from 2.4 GHz to 2 GHz owing to an increase in effective electrical length. To restore the resonance to 2.4 GHz, systematic variations in slot width and length were explored using Ansys HFSS. Optimization findings reveal that slot size tuning carefully not only restores the resonant frequency but also greatly improves impedance matching up to <tex>$\mathbf{5 1. 9 6} \boldsymbol{+} \mathbf{j 5. 4 7 9 3}$</tex> ohm, gain from <tex>$\mathbf{- 3 ~ d B}$</tex> to <tex>$\mathbf{5 ~ d B}$</tex>, and bandwidth up to 1.5104 GHz with a 63.35 percent fractional bandwidth achieved. The optimized CPW-fed antenna exhibits a good improvement in performance and is a good candidate for use in modern wireless communications.

abstract_ts: A deep learning system has been developed to convert single-phase input power into three-phase output power for operating a 10 HP induction motor. This system employs a hybrid model that exploits both LSTM and Fully Connected Neural Networks for the optimization of PWM control of a VSI. It aims to improve the efficiency of the inverter while lowering switching losses. The LSTM structure captures sequences of dependencies from the given data; therefore, it is suitable for applications in dynamic power systems, whereas the FCNN focuses on robust learning of complicated relations in control interfaces. The model was trained using data comprising voltages, currents, and frequencies in conjunction with producing the very accurate firing pulses for the inverter switches in order to maintain stability and characterized performance. Important design parameters such as voltage and current ratings and switching frequency are taken into account in order for the high-efficiency system to be attained. This will also help in the reduction of Total Harmonic Distortion, consequently improving the motor efficiency and concerns of power quality. A thorough analysis on Total Harmonic Distortion has been run to support the justification of the efficacy of the total system, all this while an inbuilt snubber circuit improves the reliability of the system. The cost-effective solution thus made is a boon for industrial and agricultural purposes where three-phase power is limited.

abstract_ts: Anxiety is an important psychological factor determining academic performance and well-being among first-year students of higher education. Thus, this study investigates the relationship of anxiety with several academic and behavioral attributes to find key contributors to anxiety and its influence on academic performance. A sample of <tex>$\mathbf{3 3 8}$</tex> freshmen was statistically analyzed with respect to Pearson correlation and T Tests and ANOVA. The results reveal moderate positive associations of anxiety with factors like sleep duration (0.3406), class participation (0.2350), and assignment completion (0.224). The increasing anxiety levels detrimentally influence student engagement. Anxiety revealed highly significant negative correlations with academic performance (-0.3837). The hypothesis tests reinforced such relationships with significant <tex>$\mathbf{p}$</tex>-values: <tex>$2.67 \times 10^{-13}$</tex> for Pearson correlation, <tex>$1.08 \times 10^{-6}$</tex> for T-Test <tex>$<0.001$</tex> and for ANOVA concerning academic performance, class participation, assignment completion, and sleep duration. The most dominant anxiety predictor determined in this study is sleep duration, and assignment completion comes next. These findings speak to the necessity for interventions specifically targeted at supporting students in the management of anxiety and resilience-building when transitioning to higher education. In summary, identifying the fundamental academic and behavioral factors pleasing to anxiety sets a basis for future works and institutional strategies for student well-being and academic success.

abstract_ts: There has been a sudden rise in post-viral neurological complications in recent times, and it is reported that at most 30 % of viral infection survivors develop early neurological symptoms. In this paper, an interpretable deep learning model is proposed that can detect such complications using brain MRI scans. Using a convolutional neural network and Grad-CAM for explainability, the model achieves 92.8 % detection rate, 94.1 % sensitivity, and 91.3 % specificity on a well-prepared post-viral MRI dataset. The system is made transparent in terms of decision-making such that clinicians can identify weak patterns associated with early-stage disorders. This model can readily be applied as an early clinical tool of assistance, especially in the post-COVID or other similar viral diseases of recent neurological presentation.

abstract_ts: Timely and accurate diagnosis of leakage faults in insulin pump sets is critical for ensuring safe and effective insulin therapy. A key challenge lies in the strong similarity between blood glucose fluctuations induced by meal intake and those caused by leakage faults, which makes reliable fault diagnosis difficult. Additionally, the scarcity of labeled leakage fault data and the significant inter-individual variability in glucose responses further complicate the development of classification models. To address these challenges, a transfer learning (TL) method based on feature alignment is proposed. Specifically, by leveraging a multilayer perceptron (MLP) neural network and incorporating local maximum mean discrepancy (LMMD) and local maximum variance discrepancy (LMVD) to align features between the source and target domains, domain shift is reduced and the ability to generalize across different subjects is enhanced. Experimental results using continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data from subjects show that our method achieves an accuracy of 92 %, significantly outperforming traditional models. The proposed transfer learning network offers promising potential for real-time fault detection in insulin pump systems, highlighting its practical applicability in clinical environments.

abstract_ts: In this paper, we investigate the aerial active reconfigurable intelligent surface (ARIS) enabled reliable and secure communications in the presence of multi-eavesdropper. Based on the phase shift configuration strategies, two aerial ARIS strategies are proposed, namely the reliability-priority (<tex>$\mathbf{R P}$</tex>) and securitypriority (SP) strategies. Considering phase estimation errors due to imperfect conditions, accurate and asymptotic closed-form expressions of the secrecy outage probability (SOP) are analyzed for the proposed two ARIS strategies to evaluate the system secrecy performance. Theoretical analysis and simulation results reveal that: 1) The SOP of the RP strategy exhibits a convex relationship with aerial ARIS height <tex>$H$</tex>, achieving a minimum at an optimal height; 2) The SP strategy consistently exhibits superior performance in terms of secrecy robustness and smoother SOP variation over <tex>$H$</tex>; 3) An increase in transmit power results in a decrease in SOP for the SP strategy, thereby emphasizing its effectiveness in degrading eavesdropper channels.

abstract_ts: Climate change poses significant risks to ecosystems, economies, and human life, making accurate climate prediction a critical research domain. Traditional climate forecasting methods, while grounded in physics-based models, often face limitations in computational complexity and adaptability to non-linear patterns. This study explores the application of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques in improving climate prediction accuracy. By analyzing data from sources like NASA, NOAA, and IMD, and employing models such as Random Forest, LSTM, and CNN, both short-term (1-7 days) and long-term (30-90 days) forecasts were generated. Evaluation metrics including RMSE, MAE, precision, and F1-score were used to assess performance. Results demonstrate that AI/ML models significantly outperform traditional models in accuracy and adaptability, especially for short-term and extreme event forecasting. However, challenges remain in integrating these data-driven approaches with physical climate models. This research advocates for hybrid modeling frameworks that leverage the strengths of both AI and physical principles.

abstract_ts: Postharvest losses in agriculture are a major challenge to food security, the economic viability of agriculture, and sustainable agricultural practices all over the globe. Any losses in quantity and quality of an agricultural produce that occur between harvest and consumption are deemed postharvest losses; and these constitute a serious challenge, especially in most developing nations. This study investigates the possible application of predictive analytics as a game changer in minimizing postharvest losses by allowing data-driven decisions along the agricultural value chain. Predictive models will assess environmental parameters in real-time supplemented by historical data on the crops and supply chain metrics to assess spoilage risk, thus optimizing storage and transport environments and timely market interventions. The study utilizes machine learning algorithms such as regression analysis, decision trees, and also time series forecasting in developing models that can predict probable loss scenarios for selected key crops; this data will include satellite imagery, weather data, storage facility records, and also the marketing trends. Results from the predictive models are measured in terms of accuracy, scalability, and usability for farmers, cooperatives, and policymakers. The research further discusses data availability, integration across heterogeneous systems, and the injustice presented to smallholder farmers due to the digital literacy divide. The results indicate that predictive analytics can plug in an early warning and actionable insight so that stakeholders can act before any spoilage occurs, which significantly brings down post-harvest losses. Some case studies from selected regions have demonstrated improvements in storage planning, supply chain coordination, and post-harvest handling practices. The paper concludes with recommendations for integrating predictive tools with agricultural extension systems and policies at the state level with due consideration for capacity building, development of digital infrastructure, and partnership with the private sector. The ultimate expectation is that predictive analytics can therefore allow for increased resilience, efficiency, and sustainability within the agricultural system.

abstract_ts: Simulations in quantum chemistry (QC) impose a heavy load on the computing power, and also entail numerous dynamically varying dependant workloads that render the issue of load balancing in a cloud environment rather significant. Although a number of load-balancing methods are usually adequate in a host of high-performance computing tasks, the identical may not hold in the case of QC tasks. This paper draws a hybrid load-balancing strategy that combines reinforcement learning and predictive modeling to find an optimal process in the allocation of resources in QC simulations on the cloud. In particular, RL is used to handle the management of systems resources whereas predictive modeling is used to predict workloads and hence restrain latency and efficiently employ cloud resources. Experiment results indicate that with the aid of ML, the framework could achieve goals in terms of the largest possible load-distribution efficiency, categorical enhancement of tts, efficiency up to 20 percent in terms of load-distribution and reduction of task-completion times. It also helps enhance the effectiveness of the QC simulation that has been conducted on the cloud infrastructure, and provides preconditions to the additional possibility of the application of ML to scientific computing.

abstract_ts: The increasing pervasiveness of mental health challenges has focused on the need of automated methods to detect psychological distress in online journals and self-expressive platforms. Conventional approaches likes lexicon based or shallow learning are not able to identify contextual nuances and evident distress signals. Some of the recent advancements have been done in the area of transfer learning and transformer-based architectures (e.g., BERT, RoBERTa, GPT models). These are strong and powerful tools for modeling the linguistic patterns which are usually complex in nature. It is now being used for the identification of subtle cues that are mostly associated with distress signals. Our study has proposed a framework showing the impact of transformer models, particularly those which are pretrained, specifically on mental health to detect psychological distress signals from online journal entries. The proposed approach mainly highlights the challenges most commonly faced in data scarcity whereas the contextual embeddings are mainly used to capture latent emotional and cognitive markers. Experimental evidence has clearly states that the transformer-based models outperforms the traditional machine learning baselines, since they are able to achieve improved accuracy, recall, and generalizability.

abstract_ts: Agriculture continues to be the backbone of many economies today, yet operating in this field still presents farmers with challenges of unpredictable weather, crop disease, lack of market access and exploitation of crops by intermediaries. In this paper, we present an intelligent digital platform ‘CropCart’; a system which supports farmers and consumers through AI crop advisory, disease diagnosis, weather prediction and e-commerce. CropCart uses machine-learning based crop recommendations, image-processing based disease detection and voice-assistance for low-literacy users. A real-time logistics module offers a means for delivery from farm to consumer ensuring transparency and fair pricing. Experimental analysis demonstrates CropCart reduces transaction or operation time by 90% and increases farmer profit margins by 25% when compared to traditional systems. Through the integration of technology in agriculture, CropCart promotes productivity, promotes sustainable farming practices and builds connections with rural farmers and modern digital markets.

abstract_ts: Automation-based light control systems have been developed in response to the increasing need for intelligent and energy-efficient building systems. The article describes the implementation of a low-power design and cadence-based smart staircase lighting system that only illuminates when a person moves on it. The motion detector and comparator circuit in the proposed architecture generates a digital control pulse, which is subsequently processed by the MOD-12 and MOD-60 synchronous counters. JK flip-flops are used to achieve precise timing control. In order to minimize needless power usage, the circuit is made to enable the staircase light to be ON for a predetermined period of time (<tex>$5-10$</tex> seconds) before turning off automatically. Cadence Virtuoso, a 45 nm CMOS transistor, was used to model, simulate, and validate this design, which provided dependable operation with little power dissipation and switching time. The suggested approach can be expanded to be incorporated into smart house and corridor lighting systems and outperforms conventional manual or microcontroller operated systems in terms of safety, automation, and energy conservation.

abstract_ts: To tackle blind search, high computational complexity, and slow convergence in traditional RRT algorithms, this paper introduces an improved algorithm, G-RRT*, which integrates goal bias and progress-based dynamic sampling. This algorithm uses a target bias strategy to directly sample the target point with a certain probability, thereby enhancing the directionality of path search. Meanwhile, it introduces a adaptive dynamic switching mechanism based on progress, which adopts target-oriented sampling to accelerate convergence when far from the target and switches to global uniform sampling to improve obstacle avoidance ability when approaching the target. Experimental results show that in complex environments, G-RRT* reduces the average number of iterations by 82 % to 87 % compared to RRT and RRT*, shortens the path length by 10 % to 20 %, and significantly improves algorithm stability. Particularly in three-dimensional space, G-RRT* demonstrates superior computational efficiency and path quality, verifying its applicability in high-dimensional complex scenarios.

abstract_ts: Globally, lung cancer is a leading contributor to mortality, accounting for an estimated 1.8 million annual deaths. To improve patient survival and provide appropriate treatment protocols, lung cancer types must be properly and promptly classified. This research makes use of the LC25000 dataset, which comprises 15,000 histopathological images classified as squamous cell carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, and benign, and carcinoma. MobileNetV2, a light convolutional neural network structure pre-trained on ImageNet with a specialized classification head composed of a 512-unit dense layer, dropout regularization, and softmax output, is the primary component of our approach. Along with the average and max pooling layers, we also tested the VGG16 and VGG19 models in comparative studies. Training was done using the Adam optimizer for each model (learning rate = 0.0001) and tested on validation metrics. The MobileNetV2 model proposed here attained a noteworthy classification accuracy of 99.7 %, surpassing VGG16 and VGG19 both in generalization and convergence rate, at the cost of a reduced model footprint. It shows the promise of MobileNetV2 as a resource efficient approach for the categorization of histological images in clinical and diagnostic scenarios

abstract_ts: This study offers an in-depth comparative evaluation of various machine learning methods for malware detection using Windows Portable Executable (PE) files. We evaluate three distinct classifiers: Random Forest, Logistic Regression, and Neural Network, trained on a dataset containing 54 statistical and behavioral features. All the Models have been trained on the similar dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that all models achieve high detection accuracy, with the neural network showing superior performance (98.2 % accuracy) compared to traditional methods. The study includes methodology, performance metrics, and analysis for each approach, providing insights into their respective strengths for malware classification tasks. In addition to accuracy, we evaluate confusion matrix & F1-score to provide a comprehensive evaluation of model performance. The analysis highlights the distinct strengths and limitations of each algorithm, offering practical insights into their suitability for malware classification tasks. These findings contribute to the development of reliable, automated malware detection systems and highlight directions for future research such as advanced feature representation and deep learning extensions.

abstract_ts: The “AI-Powered Server Log Management Software” project introduces a machine learning-based anomaly detection solution to optimize the management and analysis of server logs through the integration of artificial intelligence (AI). In the fast-paced landscape of Information Technology (IT) operations, efficient log management is crucial for identifying anomalies, preventing security threats, and ensuring the smooth functioning of server environments. The project begins by implementing an advanced log collection system that aggregates data from various server sources, capturing events, errors, and performance metrics. Leveraging machine learning algorithms, the software autonomously analyzes these logs, distinguishing between normal operational behavior and potential security incidents. The core feature of the project lies in the intelligent anomaly detection and predictive analytics capabilities. By establishing baseline patterns and continuously learning from historical log data, the system can proactively identify deviations, potential security breaches, or impending issues, enabling timely intervention by IT administrators. Moreover, the AI-Powered Server Log Management Software incorporates natural language processing (NLP) for contextual analysis, allowing IT professionals to easily interpret and derive actionable insights from complex log entries. The software also facilitates automated log categorization and prioritization, streamlining the identification of critical events that demand immediate attention. The abstract concludes by highlighting the potential benefits of the project, including improved IT operational efficiency, enhanced cybersecurity, and proactive issue resolution. As organizations increasingly rely on sophisticated server infrastructures, the AIPowered Server Log Management Software project stands as a significant advancement in IT management, empowering administrators with intelligent tools to navigate the complexities of server log analysis with precision and efficacy.

abstract_ts: PM2.5 concentration prediction is crucial for air quality management and public health protection, but its complex chemical reaction mechanisms and multi-scale spatio-temporal dependencies pose significant challenges for accurate forecasting. This paper proposes MS-PAMamba, a novel deep learning architecture that integrates multi-scale state space modeling with pollutant-aware mechanisms for enhanced PM2.5 prediction. The model comprises four core components: an input feature embedding layer, a multi-scale state space module, a pollutant-aware Mamba (PAM) mechanism, and a weather-adaptive fusion unit. The multi-scale state space module employs parallel selective state space models operating at different temporal scales (hourly, daily, weekly, seasonal), achieving linear computational complexity <tex>$\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{n})$</tex> that significantly outperforms the <tex>$\mathrm{O}\left(\mathrm{n}^{2}\right)$</tex> complexity of Transformers. The PAM mechanism explicitly models chemical reaction relationships among pollutants through multi-head self-attention, enhancing domain knowledge integration. The weather-adaptive module dynamically adjusts state space parameters based on meteorological conditions, effectively handling non-stationary pollution patterns. Comprehensive experiments on the Beijing Air Quality Monitoring dataset (2013-2017, 35,063 hourly records) demonstrate superior performance, achieving RMSE of <tex>$21.8 \mu \mathrm{g}/\mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex> and MAE of <tex>$14.2 \mu \mathrm{g}/\mathrm{m}^{3}$</tex>. Compared to state-of-the-art methods, our approach achieves 10.7 % and 13.9 % improvements in RMSE and MAE respectively, while maintaining robustness across various pollution scenarios, validating the effectiveness of multi-scale temporal modeling and domain-specific attention mechanisms in air quality prediction applications.

abstract_ts: This paper attempts to describe the involvement of gods and spirits in the creation of woodcarving arts among the Nagas. The Nagas holds great reverence for their ancestors and nature spirits, who embody the unwritten laws, traditions, and customs of their villages. Prayers were offered to these entities for good harvests, population growth, success in warfare, and protection against sickness, famine, floods, and earthquakes. The art of woodcarving, including the construction of the morung (men’s house), log-drum, and village-gate, was accompanied at every stage by a series of taboos and ritual performances. Any deviation from the prescribed methods was believed to result in calamity or even death. Despite using simple tools such as chisels, adzes, axes, and machetes (dao), and facing the numerous difficulties and insecurities of life, the Nagas’ artistic creativity thrived due to their strict adherence to rituals and taboos associated with these arts.

abstract_ts: Currently, social media (SM) platforms are among the most crucial sources of news and information. However, the spread of fake news, false information, and rumors circulated among people in various countries and regions, especially in the Arab world, presents one of the significant challenges faced by users of these platforms. They often lack credibility, transparency, and reliability. Moreover, Fake news refers to intentionally fabricated information disseminated for political, economic, or social purposes. Its spread can lead to conflicts and disputes within or outside the country. It may also propagate hate speech and racism, tarnish reputations, and so on, all of which adversely affect peacebuilding efforts within our communities. In this research study, we present a methodology for detecting fake news using Machine Learning algorithms to enhance peacebuilding efforts and mitigate the spread of fake news, reducing its negative effects on societies. We created a new dataset containing 188 short-text news articles, encompassing both fake and real news on the same topic, from social media platforms (fact-checking websites) circulated in Arab countries and the Middle East. Then, we applied four machine learning algorithms, Decision Tree (DT), Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest (RF), and Naive Bayes (NB), to the dataset. We compared our dataset with two different datasets containing fake and real news. The study results showed that the DT achieved the highest accuracy, approximately 77%, followed by NB at 74%.

abstract_ts: With the vigorous development of technologies such as big data, the Internet of Things, and artificial intelligence, smart tourism has entered a stage of in-depth reform driven by data. Smart tourism big data analysis integrates multi-source heterogeneous tourism data, becoming a key technological path to break industry information barriers, optimize resource allocation, and enhance tourist experience. This paper first collects and organizes data of Yunnan tourist attractions. Then analyzes various recommendation algorithms (user-based collaborative filtering algorithm, content-based recommendation algorithm, hybrid content and collaborative filtering algorithm, to analyze users' past behavior patterns and interest preferences, and recommend attractions that they may be interested in. Finally, experiments prove that the hybrid content and collaborative filtering model is excellent. This paper is a preliminary study of artificial intelligence in tourist attraction recommendation, and provides a reference for the upgrading of the smart tourism industry and academic research.

abstract_ts: Proper posture during exercise is often the link between achieving you did optimal results and avoiding injuries. This paper introduces a real-time posture correction system for three fundamental exercises: squats, bicep curls, and planks. Mediapipe's pose estimation framework, with the aid of some machine learning techniques, analyzes body movements to detect misalignment. With the flexibility of using either a live webcam feed or pre-recorded video files, users can interact with the system. The ultimate solution identifies key posture deviations such as knee tracking errors in squats, incorrect arm angles in bicep curls, or poor spinal alignment in planks and delivers precise, actionable feedback to help users adjust in real time. The developed tool is intuitive, adaptable, and scalable, making it suitable for individual users and fitness professionals. This work demonstrates the potential of AI-driven systems to enhance workout safety, effectiveness, and accessibility by bridging technology and exercise, by using lightweight AI models and adjustable feedback, the system provides accurate, real-time posture correction.

abstract_ts: The Internet of Things (IoT) and Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) have grown exponentially to provide unprecedented opportunities in terms of intelligent monitoring, automation of industries, and smart city implementations. Nevertheless, the fact that WSN nodes are resource-constrained and the IoT communication channels are open makes them very susceptible to replay attacks, eavesdropping, impersonation, and integrity attacks. The current cryptographic designs are strong with a high computational cost or lightweight with a weak protection with a gap on balanced designs. To solve this, we suggest a new security architecture to combine Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) to provide lightweight key establishment with Authenticated Encryption with Associated Data (AEAD) to provide secure data transmission. The methodology consists of: (i) ECC key pair authentication of nodes, and firmware verification by hash; (ii) nonces to generate new session keys and ensure freshness; (iii) hash-chained message integrity verification; and (iv) encryption and Authenticated Encryption with data to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and replay safety. Also, optional Message Authentication Codes (MACs) are added to implement the layers of defense, whereas scalability is achieved with the help of lightweight computations. The comprehensive performance analysis is carried out based on the latency, power usage, throughput and scalability at diverse network densities. Findings prove that the suggested ECC + AEAD scheme is more effective in terms of lowering the latency <tex>$\boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{8. 1 m s} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, energy usage (10.2-10.6 mJ), and throughput (114-118 kbps) than state-of-the-art cryptography tools.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a petal-inspired ultra-wideband (UWB) microstrip antenna designed for sub-terahertz 6 G applications. The antenna employs a symmetric flower-shaped structure etched on a rectangular substrate to enhance radiation efficiency and bandwidth. Using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based optimization, the design parameters were fine-tuned to maximize gain and impedance matching. Simulations were performed using Ansys HFSS, confirming excellent performance with return loss below <tex>$\mathbf{- 1 0} \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> across wide frequency bands and VSWR <tex>$<2$</tex>. The optimized design achieved peak gains of 27.3 dB and 31.4 dB at 111.15 GHz and 174.10 GHz, respectively, demonstrating high radiation directivity and minimal signal reflection. These results validate the proposed antenna's potential for high-data-rate 6 G communication systems requiring compact and high-gain configurations. The findings also emphasize the role of AIdriven optimization in next-generation antenna engineering.

abstract_ts: The rise of IoT devices in various industries has posed serious cybersecurity threats owing to the limited resource capacity and the demand of the devices to more cyber threats. Customary security products do not offer consistent protection that is adaptable and real-time. The paper suggests a cyber-attack detector framework based on machine learning that is characterized to work in an IoT setting. The system can find the threat like DDoS, spoofing, malware, and data injection with high accuracy and a low rate of false positives through the adaptation of appropriate models like LSTM and CNN. Performance tuning is also done through feature selection methods minimizing the amount of computing. In a demonstration, the ML-based system has been found to be more adaptable and faster in detection time as compared with the conventional intrusion detection system, the effectiveness of which indicates that it could be used to strengthen security in IoT.

abstract_ts: Steganography refers to hiding secret information inside regular transmission media to make it imperceptible. Traditional Least Significant Bit (LSB) techniques have been widely used to embed low-resolution secret images into higher-resolution cover images, but they suffer from limited capacity, poor robustness, and noticeable degradation of visual quality. In this paper, we present a deep learning-based approach that embeds multiple secret images into a single cover image of identical resolution. Unlike prior single-image methods, our framework employs separate encoder-decoder branches for each secret and a shared hiding network, enabling scalable multi-secret embedding. We evaluate our method on the Tiny ImageNet dataset and report both qualitative and quantitative results, including Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR), Structural Similarity Index (SSIM), and Learned Perceptual Image Patch Similarity (LPIPS). Robustness analysis under common transformations such as compression, noise, and cropping further demonstrates the viability of our approach. Comparative studies with naïve baselines and ablation experiments highlight the effectiveness of design choices such as frozen reveal networks and Gaussian noise regularization. Our results show that up to three secret images can be reliably recovered with minimal perceptual distortion, and we discuss scalability trade-offs for embedding more secrets.

abstract_ts: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) based on Transformer architectures have significantly improved capabilities in natural language processing and generation. However, deploying LLMs for inter-organizational communication poses challenges, particularly in ensuring privacy and facilitating effective collaboration. This paper introduces a novel decentralized inference meta-agent chatbot that leverages privacy-aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)-enabled LLMs for collaborative AI communication across organizations. Built on Microsoft's Autogen, the platform enables LLMs to autonomously refine responses, enhancing accuracy and relevance. It incorporates advanced hallucination mitigation techniques using Uptrain and a privacy-focused RAG frame-work that employs synthetic document generation to protect sensitive information. Comprehensive evaluations demonstrate the platform's effectiveness in maintaining contextual relevance and stringent privacy standards, effectively addressing critical challenges in LLM-enhanced collaborative AI communication. This work is a significant step toward secure and efficient inter-organizational collaboration using generative AI technologies.

abstract_ts: Malware detection and classification are of vital importance, yet the increasing use of obfuscation, polymorphism, and hybridization techniques by malware families has made this task highly challenging for cybersecurity. This study investigates behavior-based multi-class malware family classification using the large-scale BCCC-Mal-NetMem-2025 dataset. The proposed approach employs an MSFormer (Multi-Sequence Fusion Classifier), a transformer-inspired architecture designed for multi-class malware family classification through an attention mechanism, thereby harnessing the complementary strengths of network and memory modalities for more accurate and robust family-level classification. Our fusion framework integrates multi-head attention mechanisms with Word2Vec embeddings and applies SMOTE to address class imbalance, followed by a fusion layer for improved classification, unlike previous work that relied solely on a single-source dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that incorporating multiple modalities significantly improves accuracy for malware families, particularly Trojans, rootkits, and worms. These findings further highlight the importance of designing advanced malware detection models as a critical component of cyber threat defense mechanisms.

abstract_ts: The emergence of localized heating phenomena, commonly referred to as hotspots, poses a significant threat to the operational stability and energy yield of photovoltaic (PV) systems. These thermal anomalies are typically induced by cell mismatches, partial shading, or material degradation, leading to irreversible damage and long-term efficiency losses. In response to these challenges, this study introduces a robust hotspot mitigation framework empowered by artificial neural network (ANN)-based machine intelligence, aimed at proactive fault detection, thermal profiling, and power optimization in solar <tex>$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> arrays. The proposed methodology integrates thermographic data acquisition with intelligent image processing and classification techniques to detect and localize five distinct fault categories: single-cell and multi-cell hotspots, potential-induced degradation (PID), diode failures, and shadow-related anomalies. Utilizing a curated dataset of 837 annotated thermal images, the ANN-driven architecture referred to as LILNet-5 was trained and validated to deliver high precision in anomaly classification. Experimental results demonstrate that the LILNet-5 model surpasses conventional deep learning benchmarks, such as CNN, LeNet-5, and baseline ANN models, achieving an accuracy of 97 % and a notably reduced root mean square error (RMSE), thereby affirming its superiority in thermal anomaly detection. Moreover, the integration of intelligent hotspot detection with active bypass circuit logic facilitates localized fault isolation without compromising overall system continuity or inducing additional circuit complexity. The proposed approach enhances both short-term operational resilience and long-term system reliability while minimizing thermal stress, energy loss, and maintenance overhead. This research underscores the transformative role of machine intelligence in next-generation PV system diagnostics, offering a scalable and data-driven solution for real-time fault mitigation and sustainable energy harvesting.

abstract_ts: In 5G and 6G indoor environments, Visible Light Communication (VLC) is a promising wireless communication technology that enables high-speed data transmission. 2-W-PPWM and 4-M-PAWM are two hybrid modulation techniques compared in this paper with a focus on their performance in transmission data rate and PAPR. Based on the results of simulation, 4-MPAWM usually measures a higher value of 12 dB under identical conditions, while 2-W-PPWM always yields a lower PAPR with 10 dB at the fifth symbol level. Moreover, 2-W-PPWM yields better throughput with an achieved data rate of 140 Mbps compared to 4-M-PAWM's 130 Mbps. These results show how 2-W-PPWM is able to achieve a good compromise between power efficiency and high-speed transmission, making it a strong candidate for VLC applications with limited bandwidth and power sensitivity, like IoT-based smart spaces.

abstract_ts: With the recent emergence of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) technologies, there has been a growing movement advocating that AI consumer products should align with the ethically aligned trustworthy design requirements for AI driven technologies. The regulatory measures related to trustworthy AI are connected to the new European Union (EU) AI Act, which governs ethically aligned and humane design of AI enabled devices, that include modern AI wearable devices. There has been prior research towards finding security vulnerabilities within certain types of AI wearable devices. However, existing research literature indicates that these previous works have hardly studied the implications of these security issues in terms of these wearable AI devices aligning with the regulatory needs for trustworthy AI requirements. In an attempt to address this research gap, we have performed a unique experimental case study with an AI integrated smart glass and a couple of AI enabled smart watches, that are representative of the modern AI wearable devices. In this study, we have tested the AI functionalities of these wearable devices to discover security problems, and have analyzed them to assess the overall alignment of these devices with the regulatory requirements for trustworthy AI. Our initial tests show specific instances of security flaws & AI risks with these wearables that might affect users negatively and pose potential problems to the product consumers. We have carefully studied these discovered scenarios to conduct a preliminary examination of how they can potentially result in non-adherence with the regulatory criteria for trustworthy AI. Through this novel study, we overall explore the compliance of these AI wearable devices with these regulatory benchmarks, thereby determining their potential risk levels in context of user acceptability as per the new EU AI Act.

abstract_ts: In fog computing, task scheduling is a vital challenge where resource optimization and latency reduction are crucial. The dynamic priority-based scheduling methodology presented in this paper uses Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO) to distribute jobs among fog nodes in an adaptable manner. Effective and equitable task-node mapping under varying workloads is made possible by the suggested method, which combines task urgency, execution time, and resource cost into a multi-objective fitness function. When compared to traditional PSO and Genetic Algorithm, PSO with dynamic priorities consistently achieves shorter execution times, with reductions of up to <tex>$\mathbf{8 \%} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 2 \%}$</tex> across a range of task sizes, according to experimental validation using real datasets. Additionally, the architecture is extremely suitable to real-time IoT contexts since it guarantees balanced resource allocation and demonstrates resilience under abrupt workload surges. These findings support the idea that dynamic priorities might improve fog computing systems' task scheduling's flexibility, efficacy, and equity.

abstract_ts: The Internet of Things (IoT) is a versatile communication technology with potential for interconnecting multiple devices over large physical distances. Beyond the flexible connections, IoT can share and analyze the data collected in the IoT ecosystem. Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks also stand out as one of the significant threats, posing persistent breaches to the ecosystem. DDoS attacks exploit the computational power and storage resources of IoT devices. This research seeks to analyze the reasons for such attacks in IoT implementations and mitigate those attacks. To accomplish this, the study first attempts to explore different types of attacks on IoT organizations and studies the different existing mitigation methods in the literature. At the end, based on the analysis, this study proposes a new solution to mitigate DDoS attacks in IoT environments, which can also overcome storage limitations. The new solution includes two specifically designed algorithms. The first algorithm is to detect the DDoS attack source, and the second algorithm is to block the source(s) of the DDoS attack. The major advantage of implementing this new solution is that no additional hardware is required when using the algorithm at the network level. This approach helps in cost minimization and detection of attacks at the node level. The node-level implementation would also be useful in improving the scalability of the detection of such attacks. This study presents a promising and scalable approach to protect IoT organizations from DDoS attacks while simultaneously highlighting the current security issues of IoT organizations.

abstract_ts: One of the most critical steps towards early intervention and best outcomes is early detection of autism spectrum disorder (ASD). In this paper, we propose a novel attention augmented machine-learning method for accurately, interpretable, and morally ASD-prediction. We propose feature-wise ensemble learning, where individual sub-models focus on a set of communicative, social, and behavioral characteristics. Our ensemble uses a novel attention-based weighting method that dynamically gives more weight to the most important features or feature groups during prediction as opposed to traditional ensemble methods that give more weight to the most important features in a single sub-model, similar to how doctors attend to particular indications. We provide both explicit global-level and feature-level interpretability insights by integrating gradient boosting models (e.g., XGBoost and CatBoost) with feature attributions based on SHAP to promote interpretability. We also include fairnessaware learning algorithms to mitigate against demographic bias, which use causal inference methods (e.g., Causal Forests) to find factors that have a causal influence on outcomes related to ASD. Additionally, we enable real-time prediction with missing features using a dynamic feature selection module that allows for greater flexibility in clinical usage. By merging attention mechanisms with conventional machine learning, this work provides a novel approach towards the development of new clear, reliable, and highly accurate ASD-prediction tools.

abstract_ts: This paper presents a unified, hybrid graph-based recommendation framework designed to improve connection discovery in social networks and web search. Unlike traditional systems that rely only on structural rules or content similarity, this approach combines three related signals: structural connectivity (for example, Common Neighbors, PageRank), content-based similarity (cosine similarity on user profiles and TF-IDF on web page content), and community-level signals (social circles, Twitter lists, and Wikipedia categories). These signals work together through a tunable weighted ensemble, producing accurate, diverse, and understandable recommendations. The framework supports both undirected graphs (for example, Facebook) and directed graphs (for example, Twitter, Wikipedia), ensuring wide applicability. Its modular pipeline and interactive Streamlit-based interface make it flexible and easy to use. Evaluations on real-world datasets, such as Facebook EgoNet, Twitter Ego, and Wiki-TopCats, show the model's ability to provide meaningful and clear suggestions. This work improves cross-domain recommendations by bringing together social and information networks into a single, interpretable framework.

abstract_ts: Bone fractures are a frequent form of injury, necessitating precise and prompt diagnosis from X-ray images for effective treatment. Automated fracture detection systems can help radiologists by showing them areas that might be broken. This paper presents an innovative hybrid methodology for detecting bone fractures, integrating deep learning with evolutionary optimisation techniques. At first, a custom ResNet-like Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is used to get deep features from X-ray images. Focal loss and augmented data sampling techniques are used to fix class imbalance and make training harder by focussing on hard samples. After that, Particle Swarm Optimisation (PSO) is used to choose the most important subset of the extracted deep features. This lowers the number of features and makes the content more useful. An Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) is used for classification. A Genetic Algorithm (GA) optimises its hyperparameters, such as the number of hidden neurones and the activation function. This all-in-one framework makes use of the best parts of deep residual feature extraction, swarm-based feature selection, and fast ELM classification. The proposed method is tested on a publicly available X-ray bone fracture dataset and gets 98.83% accuracy on the test set, as well as high precision and recall. These results show that the method works well for finding fractures and could be useful for analysing medical images.

abstract_ts: This study maps the millet value chain in the tribal district of Nabarangpur, Odisha, with a focus on analysing livelihood outcomes, production efficiency, and market dynamics through a data-driven approach. Eight blocks of 80 farmers, processors and market actors were sampled to gather primary and secondary data to determine the important value addition points and systemic bottlenecks. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) was used to single out the most determining factors on the income of the farmers, their participation in the market and the support through the institutions. The outcomes of the PCA indicated that there are three key elements in production-profit efficiency, capitals intensity and labour-mechanization trade-offs that explained more than 90 percent of the total variation. Sorghum and finger millet became high input high output systems, pearl millet cost effective and foxtail millet was low risk low payoff crop profile. The results point to structural problems like low mechanization, slow payments and poor market connections that limit profitability. The incorporation of PCA in the value-chain analysis offered a quantitative method of revealing the relationship of dependence between the production, cost, and marketing decision. The research adds value by showing the benefits of the differentiated intervention, e.g. mechanization and support of high-performing crops and direct-to-consumer selling of cost-efficient crops, in promoting income stability, market access, and policy targeting in sustainable millet development in tribal Odisha.

abstract_ts: Fault diagnosis in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) inspection constitutes an important component for enhancing the operational efficiency of power equipment inspections. However, UAV-captured power equipment inspection images present significant challenges such as small targets, complex backgrounds, and occluded objects, which severely limit the accuracy of fault diagnosis. This paper proposes an interpretable fault diagnosis method for power equipment based on improved deep learning called Interpretable Fault Diagnosis Model with ECA and CBAM (IEC-YOLO). This method integrates four Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) modules within the backbone network to augment feature extraction capabilities in shallow layers. Simultaneously, a Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) is incorporated to suppress background interference. Furthermore, Grad-CAM++ is leveraged for model interpretability analysis, generating visual saliency maps that elucidate the decision basis underpinning diagnosis outcomes. Experimental results demonstrate that IEC-YOLO model achieves improvements of 3.1%, 2.0%, and 1.4% in precision, recall, and mean average precision (mAP50) respectively compared to the baseline model. These improvements enhance detection capabilities for small targets and occluded objects, while strengthening robustness against background interference. Additionally, the proposed framework enables pinpoint localization of fault regions in power equipment via Grad-CAM++, providing intuitive and verifiable decision support for power equipment maintenance.

abstract_ts: Local Binary Pattern (LBP) is widely used for categorizing brain tumor. Nonetheless, LBP is impacted strongly by the spurious noise. Besides, it employs a constant neighborhood size that does not accurately represent textures of different sizes or orientations. To improve the classification accuracy and overcome these shortcomings, we have utilized LBP to extract texture characteristics from brain MRI images and have generated a texture contrast map by subtracting the original image from the LBP image. This map effectively amplifies the unique textures linked to tumor regions, reducing some of the limitations of standard LBP and ultimately enhancing the precision of the brain tumor classification system. The effectiveness of our approach is demonstrated by a classification accuracy of 95.44%. This result underscores the potential of the enhanced LBP-based method, combined with k-nearest neighbours (k-NN) classification, for brain tumor categorization.

abstract_ts: DashRide is a ride-hailing system developed to address two key challenges in existing platforms: fare transparency and passenger safety. The system introduces a hybrid fare bidding model where riders can place bids within a safe range predicted by machine learning. For fare prediction, both Linear Regression and Random Forest were tested, with Random Forest achieving higher accuracy and efficiency, making it the chosen model. Safety is strengthened through Women-Only Rides, which match female passengers with verified female drivers, and an Isolation Forest model that monitors live trip data to detect suspicious patterns. A GPS-enabled emergency alert feature further ensures timely response in critical situations. Together, these innovations improve fairness in pricing and enhance trust in safety, showing how machine learning can support more reliable and equitable ride-hailing solutions.

abstract_ts: Telecom Operators must integrate advanced Control Software, robust Function and Communication Support systems, efficient B2C platforms, and flexible Middleware solutions to maintain competitive edge and service quality in the rapidly changing telecommunications landscape. Control software in contemporary telecom networks uses SDN and NFV to dynamically allocate resources, slice networks, and automate administration. Software-centric techniques provide real-time network optimization, improving performance and traffic flexibility. To handle 5G and beyond networks, Function and Communication Support systems have emerged. They currently use AI-driven predictive maintenance, SON, and modern protocol stacks to assure heterogeneous network compatibility. These systems are crucial to QoS and advanced features like ultra-reliable low-latency connectivity. Digital revolution has changed B2C telecom. Operators use big data, machine learning, and customization to improve customer experience. AI-powered chatbots, real-time billing systems, and omnichannel engagement platforms are changing customer service. Telecom middleware connects diverse systems, old infrastructure, and cutting-edge services. It supports microservices architectures, API-driven service development, and OSS/BSS integration. Advanced middleware now supports edge computing for low-latency applications and efficient data processing at network edges. The convergence of Control Software, Function and Communication Support, B2C platforms, and Middleware is driving telecom innovation. They must be integrated to enable enormous IoT, driverless cars, and smart cities with 5G and future networks. As telecommunications evolves, these components' growth and integration will shape its future. This research articles deals with various aspects of control software, function and communication support components, Business to Consumer, Middleware and their market statistics and prospective growth in today's dynamic environment.

abstract_ts: The rapid growth of Electric Vehicles (EVs) requires the identification of EV charging patterns to support effective grid management. This study systematically analyzes two public charging datasets to identify real-world EV users' charging patterns using a novel dimensionality reduction-based hybrid clustering (DRHC) approach. First, an 18-feature Individual EV Charging Profile is defined to capture technical and behavioral charging characteristics. Next, this study proposes a hybrid clustering approach for EV charging pattern identification, which combines t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) or principal component analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction, and K-means and density-based spatial clustering of applications with noise (DBSCAN) for clustering. Results demonstrate high consistency between the K-means and DBSCAN implementations of DRHC. Four distinct charging patterns are successfully identified, and typical patterns’ behaviors are straightforwardly illustrated via two-dimensional scatter plots of energy consumption versus charging duration. These findings provide crucial insights for predicting EV charging demand and supporting the sustainable integration of EVs into power grids.

abstract_ts: Multi-objective optimization involves the simultaneous optimization of conflicting objectives, posing significant challenges in engineering and scientific domains. This study presents a detailed performance evaluation of the Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) on seven standard benchmark functions, enhanced by advanced 3D Pareto front visualization techniques. The NSGA-II was implemented with improved crowding distance calculations and refined tournament selection mechanisms. Benchmark functions included three unimodal (Bent Cigar, High- Conditioned Elliptic, Rosenbrock) and four multimodal types (Rastrigin, Griewank, Ackley, HGBat). Each was optimized using a population of 50 individuals over 200 generations. Comprehensive 3D visualizations were generated to depict function landscapes and Pareto fronts, integrating fitness, diversity, and distance objectives. NSGA-II achieved near- optimal performance on unimodal functions, particularly Bent Cigar (2.52×10<sup>-15</sup>), and demonstrated precision even on ill- conditioned problems. Multimodal landscapes posed greater difficulty; Rastrigin exhibited higher median values (39.3) and standard deviations (18.5), suggesting frequent local optima entrapment. Rosenbrock displayed wide solution variance (8.14×10<sup>4</sup>), while Griewank yielded near-zero metrics consistently. The 3D visualization techniques successfully exposed trade-offs among objectives using strategic color mapping and high-resolution surface plots. This comprehensive study highlights NSGA-II's robustness across varied optimization landscapes and reveals potential areas for further enhancement in handling highly multimodal problems.

abstract_ts: Mobile ad hoc networks (MANETs) are increasing rapidly, needing QoS and security solutions with changing topology and limited resources. This examination of 40 recent publications explores sophisticated optimization-based routing, trust-aware intrusion detection, machine learning-driven anomaly categorization, and blockchain-integrated cryptography frameworks. Compared to conventional protocols, hybrid optimization models like Hybrid Sailfish-Whale Optimization with trust-aware mechanisms offer the optimal compromise between packet delivery ratio (10-15%), energy efficiency (18% gains), and end-to-end delay (15–20 ms). Blockchain-enabled technologies improve data integrity and decentralized security but cost more computationally, while deep learning methods excel at adaptive detection and traffic prediction. Machine learning, lightweight cryptographic solutions, and energy-aware routing will define resilient MANET designs for IoT, automobile ad hoc networks, and emergency response systems, the review finds.

abstract_ts: A variety of diseases, including anthracnose, powdery mildew, downy mildew, and black rot, have a significant impact on grape growth. To minimize yield loss and improve production quality, these diseases must be identified early and accurately. As time passed, a number of techniques for discovering diseases of grapes have been proposed and developed, ranging from advanced deep learning algorithms to traditional image processing. The accuracy, strength, and current time use of these methods are compared and discussed in this paper. likewise, a novel method based on transformer-based computational structure and multimodal data fusion is created to address current issues and enhance disease detection performance in early stage with going forward.

abstract_ts: This paper presents the design, modeling, and optimization of a high-frequency axial rotary transformer for wireless power transmission in self-excited synchronous traction motors for electric vehicles. Traditional self-excited synchronous motors suffer from significant reliability issues due to mechanical brushes and slip rings used for rotor excitation. To overcome this limitation, a rotary transformer-based contactless excitation system operating at 33.8 kHz is proposed. Ferrite core material is chosen due to its low eddy current losses, thermal stability, and high permeability at high frequencies. Analytical modeling was performed to calculate leakage and mutual inductance forming the basis for the equivalent circuit models. The design was further validated using finite element analysis in ANSYS Maxwell. A Screening-Based optimization algorithm was employed to minimize copper losses while ensuring adequate voltage and current levels. The optimal design achieved with 10 primary and 15 secondary turns demonstrated effective power transmission and thermal robustness.

abstract_ts: This article introduces AgriBot: A Monitoring System for Agriculture and Alert System, which can be used in smart farming and precision agriculture processes. It combines four-wheel robotic platform and environmental sensors that include the DHTll capable of measuring temperature and humidity and MQ135 that measures air quality. The main component of the system is constructed around the NodeMCU ESP8266 microcontroller that processes sensor data and communicates in real time with the cloud with the help of Firebase Realtime Database. The goal of Agribot is to enhance the monitoring of crops as well as minimizing manual labor through a mobile lOT enabled system. Validation of performance is done by field testing, which displayed the operation of sensor's reliably with some minor fluctuations about <tex>$\pm 1.5^{\circ}\mathrm{C}-\pm 3^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$</tex>. It demonstrated responsive, and proper command execution while navigating.

abstract_ts: In this paper, we develop a new method of Emotion- Aware and Cognitive-State-Sensitive Retrieval-Augmented Gen-eration (RAG) chatbot that is supplemented with Explainable AI (XAI) features. It is able to dynamically adapt to User responses based on their emotions (extracted through real-time sentiment analysis) namely happiness, sadness, anger, confusion. It also incorporates a cognitive state module as a way of reviewing and adapting to the curiosity of the user, engagement, or confusion. As well as being traceable and source-attributed in their answer, the system can perform Theory-of-Mind reasoning by means of a Belief Alignment Score to assess the differences in understanding between the user and the agent. Unlike prior research that has ex-plored (and isolated) each component, we have composed emotion awareness, counterfactual feedback, belief tracking, and adaptive memory in synergy to provide a highly personalized and human- friendly form of AI response. Experimental analysis demonstrates that it has better trust and user interaction and interpretability when compared to conventional black-box chatbots. The study paves the way to the subsequent-generation AI interface in delicate fields like in finance, teaching, and psychology.

abstract_ts: The registration of optical images and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images is a challenging technology in the field of earth observation remote sensing. By precisely registering images obtained from different sensors in the same area, it lays the foundation for supporting all-weather remote sensing applications. Traditional methods utilize the consistency of phase and gradient to extract common manual features for optical and SAR image registration. However, there is a significant nonlinear radiation difference between optical images and SAR images, which seriously restricts the accuracy and precision of image registration. To improve the accuracy of image registration, an efficient feature registration and position registration algorithm based on local depth feature descriptors is adopted for image registration. Depth feature descriptors are generated through a complex deep convolutional neural network composed of multiple dense convolutional blocks and cross-stage partial networks to achieve position registration of SAR images and optical images. At the same time, the Delaunay triangulation method and the piecewise affine distortion algorithm are combined to affine the SAR image onto the optical image, achieving the fusion of the optical image and the SAR image. The experimental results show that the dense convolutional network has a good effect in feature extraction, especially in urban area feature extraction. Meanwhile, the method of SAR and optical image feature registration and fusion based on deep learning has excellent matching and fusion effects, with all results within one pixel, which can provide favorable support for environmental dynamic monitoring, target dynamic changes and military reconnaissance.

abstract_ts: The inversion accuracy of key parameters for the Probability Integral Method, a core technique in mining subsidence prediction, is often hindered by small datasets and the poor generalization of shallow neural networks. To overcome these limitations, we propose a Mixture of Experts (MOE) deep learning model featuring a Mamba-based feature fusion mechanism for high-precision parameter inversion. Our model integrates a trio of heterogeneous experts-CNN, Transformer, and a regressionadapted prototype network-within an MOE architecture. Subsequently, a novel Mamba fusion module is introduced for the efficient deep integration of expert-generated features. The proposed model demonstrates superior performance, achieving a Willmott’s Index of Agreement (WIA) of 0.9035, significantly surpassing baseline models. Ablation studies further reveal a strong synergy between the MOE framework and the Mamba module. This work establishes a new and effective paradigm for parameter inversion in mining subsidence prediction.

abstract_ts: The extinction of the quagga is examined through colonial taxonomy, Indigenous ǀXam cosmology, and modern conservation efforts. This chapter follows the animal’s transition from an ecological agent in ǀXam traditions to a scientific specimen in European museums and Wunderkammern. The analysis criticizes Carl Linnaeus’s classification system as a “Linnaean Madrasa”—a knowledge institution that imposes European categories on African biodiversity while erasing Indigenous ways of knowing. The chapter also explores Wunderkammern aesthetics and contemporary de-extinction efforts. By analyzing preservation technologies that turned the quagga from a living creature into a static exhibit, the chapter illustrates how museum practices aestheticize extinction. The story of the quagga acts as a lens to understand the ethical failures of preservation without context and emphasizes the importance of “convivencia memory.”

abstract_ts: The abstract nature of data structures and algorithms remains a challenge in computer science education. This study aims to design and develop an educational tool based on metaverse and virtual reality technologies to support the teaching and learning of data structures and algorithms. We adopted a Design Science Research methodology, involving the development of an immersive learning tool available in two formats: a virtual reality version and a metaverse version. To evaluate the tool, questionnaires were administered at two distinct moments. Initially, a group of forty students assessed the tool, focusing on the didactic effectiveness of the experience alongside an usability survey. Subsequently, a second group of eight students was surveyed to examine both usability and immersion aspects of the tool. As a result, the tool demonstrated significant gains in conceptual understanding for undergraduate students (p < 0.05), particularly with respect to stacks and queues. Usability assessments, using the System Usability Scale, yielded positive scores (80 and 75.3), suggesting ease of use. However, the Igroup Presence Questionnaire score of 2.92 indicated a lower sense of immersion, which could impact user engagement. These findings suggest that, while the tool effectively enhances conceptual understanding, interactive elements need refinement to optimize immersion and practical problem-solving transfer. The study provides an evaluated immersive tool and empirical evidence with potential implications for virtual learning environments. Future work should focus on addressing immersion gaps and improving practical application transfer to further consolidate the proposed contribution.

abstract_ts: Despite its importance for immersion in virtual reality (VR), multisensory integration, particularly olfaction, remains underexplored. This study addresses this by developing AI computational models that analyze tri-channel physiological biofeedback (eye tracking, ECG, and GSR) to quantify the sense of presence in VR. Our methodology focuses on interpreting emotional and attentional states from these multimodal signals using supervised learning and explainable AI. Validated using the VREED dataset, which includes synchronized physiological and behavioral data from 360° immersive experiences, this work lays the groundwork for future integration into the SOFIA System. SOFIA is an olfactory-enhanced VR platform that synchronizes scent delivery with user gaze and depth. By enabling real-time emotion classification and presence estimation from physiological parameters, this research empowers the development of adaptive, personalized VR systems. This, in turn, opens new frontiers for more immersive, therapeutically effective, and tailored multisensory environments for digital well-being.

abstract_ts: Semantic segmentation tasks have become a core component in a wide range of computer vision applications, ranging from face segmentation for access security, to customized goodies distributing in e-gamming or unmanned car navigation. As this methodological framework keeps evolving, the need to track the intellectual property rights for semantic segmentation neural networks arises. Watermarking is an appealing solution in this respect, as it already proved its effectiveness in solving similar concerns related to multimedia content. The present paper reports on a study on the use of watermarking for NN-based face segmentation models and their inferences. From the methodological point of view, a watermarking solution is advanced and subsequently instantiated for covering the needs of two watermarking insertion strategies, namely post-training white-box and encapsulated black-box. Beyond its applicative utility, this also shows how synergies between image and neural network watermarking can be established. The experimental results consider the imperceptibility and robustness properties, evaluated for 4 face segmentation datasets (CelebA, LaPa, LFW, HELEN), and 3 NN models (DeepLab, FCN, and LRASPP), thus serving as a proof of concepts for NN watermarking for face segmentation.

abstract_ts: Breast cancer remains a leading cause of death among women worldwide, impacting people of all ages. Early and accurate detection significantly influences treatment outcomes and patient survival rates. Recent advances in computer vision and image analysis have made highly accurate breast cancer diagnosis feasible, particularly when combined with high-resolution mammography imaging and robust computational power. As the burden shifts from humans to machines, energy consumption for inference becomes a critical concern amid a high workload. However, the complexity of these advanced models often makes their decision-making processes vague, creating challenges for medical professionals who rely on explainable results, especially during high-pressure clinical situations. To address this issue, our study evaluates the energy consumption and explainability of three state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks (CNN) and Vision Transformer models commonly used for breast cancer detection. Additionally, we explore the effectiveness of Global Response Normalization (GRN) in enhancing model explainability by mimicking transformer-like behavior within CNN. We further introduce Hybrid Class Activation Mapping (Hybrid-CAM), an innovative visualization technique combining global channel importance with detailed spatial activations, offering clearer and more intuitive explanations compared to traditional methods based solely on per-pixel gradients to salient cancerous lesions. Our findings demonstrate that Transformer-based architectures do not necessarily outperform CNN models in breast cancer detection tasks. Ultimately, we emphasize that simpler architectures with enhanced explainability can significantly streamline the decision-making process for medical radiologists, fostering greater trust and efficiency in clinical settings.

abstract_ts: This paper presents the experimental study on acoustic performance of the vertical axial fan. The axial flow fan is arranged in a vertical low speed wind tunnel with interchangeable open-jet and solid-wall test section. The noise of the fan has the characteristics of dipole sound source, and the fundamental blade passing frequency harmonic or 1BPF and the 2BPF harmonic are the most important parts of the fan aerodynamic noise. The 1BPF harmonic and 2BPF harmonic of the fan are obviously attenuated after passing through the fan stator blades, which verifies the BPF cut-off design effect of the fan. The test results of the fan with different operation conditions were obtained by changing the combination of the rotor and the stator, the test section of the vertical wind tunnel and the incoming flow condition of the fan were discussed. The test results show that with the same inlet flow conditions, the discrete noise value of the fan is determined by the rotating speed, and the broadband noise is more related to the fan power. The deterioration of the inlet flow conditions can lead to a significant increase in the discrete noise and broadband noise of the fan. The research results of this paper can provide data support for the acoustic design of vertical axial fan in the next step.

abstract_ts: It is essential to perform fault acoustic source localization (FASL) for the health maintenance of mechanical systems under strong noise and multiple interference environments. While sparse optimization methods offer high localization accuracy and excellent generalization, their performance heavily depends on manually tuned hyper-parameters. On the other hand, deep neural network can learn network parameters from training data to perform fault localization directly, but its generalization capability and physical interpretability are weak. To address these issues, a hyper-Parameter Learning Network (PLN) is proposed to perform high-accuracy fault source localization rapidly and adaptively. The PLN integrates sparse optimization with learnable hyper-parameters (step size and shrinkage threshold). These parameters are trained in a end-to-end way via MSE minimization, which combines model-driven robustness with data-driven adaptability. Simulations and experiments show that the PLN significantly improves both localization accuracy and convergence speed over sparse methods, and provides an efficient and scalable solution for FASL in complex environments.

abstract_ts: The detection of Code Smells is a critical aspect of maintaining software quality, as it helps identify areas in the code that may lead to technical debt and decreased maintainability. Over the past decade, researchers have increasingly focused on Code Smells hybrid detection, which combine characteristics of multiple traditional Code Smells, presenting unique challenges for detection and its optimization. This paper provides a literature review of the advancements in Code Smells detection techniques over the last ten years. We analyze various detection methods, including static analysis, machine learning, and heuristic-based approaches, highlighting their strengths and limitations. Through this review, we identify key trends, challenges, and gaps in the current research landscape. In particular, the rapid development of Android programming is not accompanied by Android-based code smell detection. Our findings suggest that while significant progress has been made, there remains a need for more robust and adaptable detection methods that can handle the complexity of Code Smells in diverse software environments including Android source code. Finally, we propose directions for future research using Random Forest and Particle Swarm Optimization added with Levy Flight approach is used iteratively to find the combination of Random Forest parameters. This review aims to serve as a optional valuable resource for researchers and practitioners seeking to enhance software quality through improved hybrid detection on Code Smells.

abstract_ts: Orthostatic hypotension (OH), caused by efferent baroreflex failure, can lead to syncope and is associated with high mortality rates among individuals with neurodegenerative diseases. Several studies in recent years have aimed to estimate baroreflex sensitivity (BRS) during orthostatic stressors using measures of cardiovascular coupling (CVC): the degree of synchronization between time series cardiovascular signals. However, these efforts have relied on blood pressure sensing using bulky, wired setups, and the majority have only quantified changes in CVC during or after the occurrence of OH. In this study, we characterized CVC at rest in <tex>$N=26$</tex> participants (20 with a neurodegenerative disease) using a chest-worn patch that recorded electrocardiogram (ECG) and photoplethysmogram (PPG) signals. From the ECG and PPG data recorded during a 5-minute supine rest period prior to an orthostatic challenge, we derived interbeat interval (IBI) and PPG amplitude (PPG <tex>$_{\text{amp }}$</tex>) time series features as indices of cardiac rhythm and vascular function, respectively. We then quantified the coupling between IBI and <tex>$\text{PPG}_{\text{amp }}$</tex> using time delay stability (TDS). Following an active standing test, 12 participants experienced OH. We found that mean TDS during the rest period was 22.9% lower in the OH group than in the no-OH group <tex>$(p<\text{0. 0 1})$</tex>. Furthermore, we found that resting TDS was moderately correlated with the change in systolic blood pressure from supine to standing (<tex>$\rho=0.43, p<$</tex> 0.05). Thus, we demonstrated the effectiveness of a multimodal wearable in capturing a marker of impaired resting CVC prior to OH occurrence. This work enables the deployment of wearable sensing for estimating BRS to assist with early screening of autonomic dysfunction in the future.

abstract_ts: Early dementia detection is a global healthcare priority in diverse populations. In this study, we propose a language-agnostic screening pipeline for dementia detection in the early stage. First, we use speaker diarization to isolate the speech of the target subject from a conversational recording. From the extracted speech segments, we derive a set of acoustic features (e.g., spectral centroid, pitch mean, mel-frequency cepstral coefficients) and linguistic features (e.g., normalized tone contrast, articulation clarity coefficient, articulatory effort coefficient). These features are used to train a ResNet-based binary classifier to distinguish between Healthy Controls (HC) and individuals with Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI). We evaluated the trained model on a held-out test set comprising speakers of previously unseen languages, achieving an accuracy of 70%. This cross-lingual transfer performance highlights the potential of our approach for scalable, language-independent dementia screening.
